articles.title: Iran attacks five Gulf nations, shuts Hormuz after US bombing: All to know
articles.summary: Tehran hits back at several GCC countries, following the most intense US bombing since an MoU was signed last month.
articles.titleZh: 伊朗袭击五个海湾国家，在美国轰炸后关闭霍尔木兹：众所周知
articles.summaryZh: 继美国上个月签署谅解备忘录以来最猛烈的轰炸之后，德黑兰对几个海湾合作委员会国家进行了反击。

contentParagraphs.en: Iran has mounted attacks on Gulf states and declared the Strait of Hormuz closed after the United States conducted its third round of strikes in a week, in a serious escalation as the ongoing conflict spirals.Tehran on Sunday claimed attacks on Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan, Qatar and Oman, calling them its response to renewed US bombings on cities along its southern coast.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4US attacks Iran overnight; Tehran targets US sites in the regionlist 2 of 4Hormuz shipping in focus as Iran-US rhetoric heats uplist 3 of 4Trump trades threats with Iran’s leader, Araghchi holds talks with Omani FMlist 4 of 4Trump administration subpoenas New York Times reporters over coverageend of listThe widescale US strikes came after Iran shut the Strait of Hormuz — a critical waterway and one of the biggest flashpoints in the conflict — accusing Washington of violating a memorandum of understanding (MoU) signed between the two sides last month.So, where is the conflict headed? Here is everything we know.Why has Iran attacked Gulf states and closed Hormuz?Iran launched missile and drone attacks targeting US military bases and facilities in several Gulf states, while the US Central Command (CENTCOM) carried out a third round of strikes targeting radar, missile, and drone sites across southern Iran last week.The US attacks came after Iran opened fire on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz and announced the closure of the strategic waterway until further notice, with one crew member missing, according to CENTCOM.Iran’s powerful parliament speaker and key peace negotiator, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, said on Sunday, “The era of one-sided deals is over.”“We told you: keep your word or pay the price. Reality is knocking,” Ghalibaf posted on X with an image of Article 5 of the MoU, which relates to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.On Wednesday, US President Donald Trump announced that the ceasefire with Iran was over. His statement was followed by Iran’s Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei pledging to avenge his father’s killing.How did we reach here?The fragile MoU reached between the US and Iran had several glaring gaps, keeping the door to escalation ajar.The tensions spilled over into the Strait of Hormuz again last Monday, when Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) struck three commercial vessels, including a Qatari liquefied natural gas (LNG) tanker off the coast of Oman.The next day, the US carried out strikes on Iranian military targets, and Tehran responded with missile and drone attacks on US bases across the Gulf, prompting Trump to call off the ceasefire.The tit-for-tat attacks continued. On Saturday night, the IRGC announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz until further notice after attacking a container ship using what it called an unapproved route. On Sunday, a second vessel on the strait was hit.Where did the latest US strikes hit?CENTCOM said its third round of strikes on Iran last week was “holding Iranian forces accountable” for their recent attack on a Cyprus-flagged ship in the Strait of Hormuz.It said it hit about 140 military targets that “included Iranian missile and drone sites, naval capabilities, ammunition storage facilities, communication networks, and coastal surveillance locations”.It added that more than 300 targets were struck over the course of three nights throughout the week “to degrade Iran’s ability to attack civilian mariners and commercial vessels freely transiting the strait”.Iran’s state broadcaster IRIB said the US launched air attacks on the outskirts of the city of Veysian, in the western Lorestan province, while another strike hit a military base in Iran’s Khondab.Officials from Bushehr, on Iran’s southern coast, told local media that US forces attacked five cities in the province, including Asaluyeh, Dir, Bushehr, Dashti and Tangestan.Tehran has said the loss of lives and the extent of damage are under review.Where did Iran hit back overnight?Since the start of the ongoing conflict in late February, Tehran has accused the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries of actively supporting US military operations by hosting its bases and allowing it to use their airspace.OmanThe IRGC claimed a “heavy and surprise” attack on logistics support centres and refuelling platforms used by US aircraft carriers at the port of Duqm in Oman, according to IRIB.The IRGC’s public relations office told IRIB the sites were “destroyed” in the attack.QatarThe IRGC said it also targeted Qatar’s Al Udeid airbase with ballistic missiles and claimed to have destroyed a fighter plane maintenance centre, as well as a command-and-control centre at the base.Qatar’s Ministry of Defence said it intercepted incoming Iranian fire. Three people, including a child, were wounded as a result of falling shrapnel from the interception of Iranian attacks, Qatar’s Ministry of Interior said.KuwaitIran’s army said it used explosive drones to target a Patriot air defence system, an ammunition depot and a radar site belonging to the US military in Kuwait.BahrainIn another wave of drone attacks, Tehran targeted a US communications system and radar site in Bahrain.JordanThe IRGC said it targeted US military facilities at Prince Hassan airbase in Jordan with several ballistic missiles, and claimed to have destroyed a command-and-control centre at the base, as well as hangars housing MQ-9 drones.What’s happening in the Strait of Hormuz?Iran has closed down the strait after firing a warning shot that struck a vessel travelling on an unapproved route, and said on Sunday it had disabled a second vessel.The strait will remain closed until “the end of US interference in this region”, the IRGC said.Iranian officials told state media the US military has been trying to create an “illegal route” through the Strait of Hormuz, causing insecurity in the area.The narrow-yet-vital waterway — touted as the artery of global trade, hosting 20 percent of energy flow — has been at the
contentParagraphs.zh: 在美国进行一周内的第三轮袭击后，伊朗对海湾国家发动了袭击，并宣布关闭霍尔木兹海峡，随着持续的冲突不断升级，局势严重升级。德黑兰周日声称对巴林、科威特、约旦、卡塔尔和阿曼发动袭击，称其是对美国再次轰炸其南部沿海城市的回应。推荐故事列表 4 项内容列出美国隔夜袭击伊朗的 4 起事件中的 1 起；德黑兰瞄准该地区的美国地点list 4 中的 2 霍尔木兹航运成为焦点，伊朗与美国的言论升温 4 中的 3 特朗普与伊朗领导人进行贸易威胁，阿拉格奇与阿曼 FM 举行会谈 清单 4 中的 4 特朗普政府向《纽约时报》记者发出传票的传票列表结束美国的大规模打击是在伊朗关闭霍尔木兹海峡之后发生的，霍尔木兹海峡是一条重要的水道，也是中东地区最大的爆发点之一冲突——指责华盛顿违反了双方上个月签署的谅解备忘录（MoU）。 那么，冲突将走向何方？这就是我们所知道的一切。伊朗为何袭击海湾国家并关闭霍尔木兹？伊朗对多个海湾国家的美国军事基地和设施发动导弹和无人机袭击，而美国中央司令部(CENTCOM)上周对伊朗南部的雷达、导弹和无人机基地进行了第三轮袭击。据中央司令部称，美国发动袭击之前，伊朗向霍尔木兹海峡的商船开火，并宣布关闭战略水道，直至另行通知，其中一名船员失踪。强大的议长兼重要和平谈判代表穆罕默德·巴格尔·加利巴夫 (Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf) 周日表示，“单方面交易的时代已经结束。”“我们告诉过你：遵守诺言，否则付出代价。现实正在敲响。”加利巴夫在 X 上发布了谅解备忘录第 5 条的图片，该条款涉及霍尔木兹海峡的重新开放。周三，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普宣布与伊朗的停火协议结束。伊朗最高领袖哈梅内伊发表声明后，承诺为父亲的杀戮报仇。我们是怎么走到这一步的？美国和伊朗之间达成的脆弱谅解备忘录存在几个明显的漏洞，使升级之门半开。上周一，伊朗伊斯兰革命卫队 (IRGC) 袭击了三艘商船，其中包括一艘卡塔尔液化天然气 (LNG) 油轮，紧张局势再次蔓延到霍尔木兹海峡第二天，美国对伊朗军事目标进行打击，德黑兰则以导弹和无人机袭击海湾地区的美国基地作为回应，促使特朗普取消停火。针锋相对的袭击仍在继续。周六晚上，伊斯兰革命卫队在使用所谓未经批准的航线袭击一艘集装箱船后，宣布关闭霍尔木兹海峡，直至另行通知。周日，海峡上的第二艘船只被击中。美国最新的袭击袭击了哪里？中央司令部表示，上周对伊朗的第三轮袭击是为了“追究伊朗军队最近在霍尔木兹海峡袭击一艘塞浦路斯国旗的船只的责任”。它说，袭击了大约 140 个军事目标，其中“包括伊朗导弹和无人机基地、海军能力、弹药储存设施、通信网络和沿海监视地点”。它补充说，超过 300 个目标一周内的三个晚上对目标进行了袭击，“以削弱伊朗攻击自由通过海峡的民用海员和商船的能力”。伊朗国家广播公司IRIB表示，美国对洛雷斯坦省西部韦西安市的郊区发动了空袭，而另一次袭击则袭击了伊朗孔达布的一个军事基地。伊朗南部海岸布什尔的官员告诉当地媒体，美军袭击了五个城市该省包括阿萨卢耶省、迪尔省、布什尔省、达什提省和坦吉斯坦省。德黑兰表示正在审查人员伤亡和损害程度。伊朗一夜之间在哪里进行反击？自2月底持续冲突开始以来，德黑兰指责海湾合作委员会国家通过托管其基地并允许其使用其领空来积极支持美国的军事行动。阿曼伊斯兰革命卫队声称对后勤支持中心和基地组织发动了“严重而突然”的袭击。据IRIB报道，美国航母在阿曼杜库姆港使用的加油平台。IRGC的公共关系办公室告诉IRIB，这些地点在袭击中被“摧毁”。卡塔尔IRGC表示，它还用弹道导弹瞄准了卡塔尔的乌代德空军基地，并声称摧毁了战斗机维修中心以及该基地的指挥和控制中心。卡塔尔国防部表示，它拦截了来自伊朗的火力。卡塔尔内政部称，拦截伊朗袭击时掉落的弹片造成三人受伤，其中包括一名儿童。科威特伊朗军队表示，他们使用爆炸性无人机瞄准了驻科威特美军的爱国者防空系统、弹药库和雷达站。巴林在另一波无人机袭击中，德黑兰瞄准了美国在巴林的通信系统和雷达站。约旦伊斯兰革命卫队称，他们的目标是美国在普林斯的军事设施。约旦哈桑空军基地发射了多枚弹道导弹，并声称摧毁了该基地的指挥控制中心以及装有 MQ-9 无人机的机库。霍尔木兹海峡发生了什么事？伊朗在向一艘在未经批准的航线上行驶的船只发出警告后关闭了该海峡，并于周日表示已使第二艘船只瘫痪。该海峡将保持关闭，直到“美国对该地区的干预结束”。伊朗革命卫队表示。 伊朗官员告诉官方媒体，美国军方一直试图在霍尔木兹海峡开辟一条“非法路线”，导致该地区不安全。这条狭窄但至关重要的水道——被誉为全球贸易大动脉，承载着 20% 的能源流——一直处于危险之中。

articles.title: Datacentres drive up big tech’s carbon emissions to a third of those of France
articles.summary:
Microsoft, Amazon and Google say they still aim to achieve net zero output despite construction boom
Microsoft, Amazon and Google’s collective carbon emissions have increased by nearly a fifth in the past year, driven largely by datacentre construction.
In the financial year endi
articles.titleZh: 数据中心使大型科技公司的碳排放量达到法国的三分之一
articles.summaryZh:
微软、亚马逊和谷歌表示，尽管建筑业蓬勃发展，但他们仍致力于实现净零产出
去年，微软、亚马逊和谷歌的集体碳排放量增加了近五分之一，这主要是由数据中心建设推动的。
在财政年度结束时

contentParagraphs.en: Microsoft, Amazon and Google’s collective carbon emissions have increased by nearly a fifth in the past year, driven largely by datacentre construction.In the financial year ending March 2026, the three tech companies emitted 119m mTCO₂e (metric tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent), or about a third of those of France.The previous year, they emitted roughly 101m mTCO₂e, roughly equivalent to the 2024 emissions of Czechia.The US companies’ climate ambitions have been hit in recent years by a boom in demand for cloud services, such as storing data or running servers over the internet, related to training and operating chatbots and other AI products.Cecilia Rikap, an economics professor at University College London, said: “Claims by Microsoft, Amazon and Google about their clouds being ecologically friendly and sustainable are a marketing strategy. Governments should remember these expanding carbon footprints when the very same companies offer addressing the ecological crisis with AI solutions.“And, as migration to their clouds expands, and companies store data and train and use AI models and all sorts of digital technologies, these other companies are outsourcing their own digital/AI carbon footprint to cloud giants. Basically, shifting to the cloud helps other corporations obscure their environmental footprint.”Microsoft, Google and Amazon were contacted for comment.These increases were documented in the companies’ annual sustainability reports, which they have released over the past weeks. In its report released on Thursday, Microsoft said its carbon emissions had increased by 25% over the past year to 20m mTCO₂e, “driven primarily by the expansion of our datacentre infrastructure”.Google said its emissions had increased 18% over the past year, “driven by increases in supply chain activities that supported the rapid expansion of our business”. The search company says its AI systems have come up with solutions that have already helped to reduce emissions elsewhere by 41m tonnes of CO2 last year.Amazon reported a 16% increase in emissions overall, and a 20% increase in supply chain emissions, which included datacentre building and construction. In its report, it still framed this as “making progress” towards its goal of net zero emissions in 2040.skip past newsletter promotionafter newsletter promotionThe bulk of these emissions come from a big, global push to build the infrastructure for artificial intelligence. The world’s biggest tech companies are on track to spend $765bn (£570bn) this year, mostly on building AI datacentres – in locations from Norway to North Tyneside.It is a drastic reversal in a years-long push by big technology companies to cut their carbon emissions. Prior to this year, Microsoft’s emissions appeared to have flatlined, at 16m mTCO₂e, in 2023 and 2024. All three companies still say they aim to achieve net zero emissions: Google and Microsoft by 2030, Amazon by 2040.“The increases in total carbon emissions are strongly correlated with [the companies’] AI investment,” said Shaolei Ren, a professor of electrical engineering at University of California, Riverside. He noted that Microsoft’s sustainability report also suggested that there were fewer carbon credits available on global markets to offset its emissions. “While companies are actively investing in or purchasing carbon credits, the figure suggests a possible lack of credit supply in the carbon market to meet the technology companies’ needs … Everyone is talking about the lack of physical goods and infrastructure like power, but there may also be a lack of virtual goods – carbon credits.”Proposals for building datacentres across the global tech sector are becoming more numerous and ambitious as demand for AI tools, and investment by AI companies in the models that underpin them, increases. JLL, a US property consultancy, expects about 1,200 datacentres to be built globally between now and 2030, with demand overwhelmingly driven by AI.The datacentre boom is accompanied by vast projected power demands. The Uptime Institute, which rates and inspects datacentres, estimates that big datacentre projects announced last year would consume 1.3% of the world’s electricity usage, or a near-doubling of current datacentre demand. The majority of that new power demand will come from US projects, it said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 微软、亚马逊和谷歌的总体碳排放量在过去一年中增加了近五分之一，这主要是由数据中心建设推动的。在截至 2026 年 3 月的财年中，这三家科技公司的排放量为 1.19 亿吨二氧化碳当量，约为法国的三分之一。上一年，它们的排放量约为 1.01 亿吨二氧化碳当量，大致相当于捷克 2024 年的排放量。美国公司的气候雄心在 2020 年受到了打击。近年来，对云服务的需求激增，例如存储数据或通过互联网运行服务器，与培训和操作聊天机器人和其他人工智能产品相关。伦敦大学学院经济学教授塞西莉亚·里卡普(Cecilia Rikap)表示：“微软、亚马逊和谷歌声称他们的云生态友好且可持续，这是一种营销策略。当这些公司提供人工智能解决方案解决生态危机时，政府应该记住这些不断扩大的碳足迹。“而且，随着云迁移的扩大，公司存储数据和培训并使用人工智能模型和各种数字技术，这些其他公司正在将自己的数字/人工智能碳足迹外包给云巨头。基本上，转向云有助于其他公司掩盖他们的环境足迹。”我们联系了微软、谷歌和亚马逊征求意见。这些增长记录在这些公司过去几周发布的年度可持续发展报告中。微软在周四发布的报告中表示，其碳排放量在过去一年中增加了 25%，达到 2000 万 mTCO2e，“主要是由于我们数据中心基础设施的扩张所致”。谷歌表示，在过去一年中，其排放量增加了 18%，“是由于供应增加所致” ”。这家搜索公司表示，其人工智能系统已经提出了解决方案，去年已经帮助其他地方减少了 4100 万吨二氧化碳排放。亚马逊报告称，总体排放量增加了 16%，供应链排放量增加了 20%，其中包括数据中心的建设和建设。在其报告中，它仍然将其视为朝着 2040 年净零排放目标“取得进展”。其中大部分排放量来自于全球范围内大力推动的人工智能基础设施建设，今年全球最大的科技公司预计将斥资 7650 亿美元（5700 亿英镑），主要用于在从挪威到北泰恩赛德等地建设人工智能数据中心。今年之前，微软的排放量似乎已趋于平稳，为 1600 万 mTCO2e。 2023 年和 2024 年。这三家公司仍然表示，他们的目标是到 2030 年实现净零排放：谷歌和微软到 2040 年实现净零排放。加州大学河滨分校电气工程教授 Shaolei Ren 表示，“碳排放总量的增加与这些公司的人工智能投资密切相关。”他指出，微软的可持续发展报告还表明，全球市场上可用于抵消其排放的碳信用额减少了。 “随着对人工智能工具的需求以及人工智能公司对支持这些工具的模型的投资的增加，全球科技行业建立数据中心的提案变得越来越多和雄心勃勃。美国房地产咨询公司仲量联行预计将建立约 1,200 个数据中心。从现在到 2030 年，全球范围内的数据中心繁荣将伴随着巨大的预计电力需求。该研究所负责对数据中心进行评估和检查，预计去年宣布的大型数据中心项目将消耗全球用电量的 1.3%，即当前数据中心需求的近一倍，其中大部分新电力需求将来自美国项目。

articles.title: How Aldi is taking on US supermarkets with its $4 almond butter
articles.summary: The German supermarket's $9bn US push targets urban hubs like Manhattan. Can its discount model match Walmart?
articles.titleZh: Aldi 如何用 4 美元的杏仁黄油挑战美国超市
articles.summaryZh: 这家德国超市在美国投入 90 亿美元，目标是曼哈顿等城市中心。其折扣模式能与沃尔玛媲美吗？

contentParagraphs.en: Image source, Francisco Velasquez/BBCImage caption, Mary Porter shops at Aldi's new Manhattan location in New York City for the first timeByFrancisco VelasquezBusiness reporterWhen Mary Porter walked into Manhattan's newest Aldi store hunting for bargains, the long-time resident found what she considered a retail miracle in plain sight: a $4 jar of almond butter that costs $22 in her own neighbourhood. "Aldi has the reputation for being inexpensive, so I thought I would come and check it out, and by golly, it is amazing," Porter, 79, told the BBC, marvelling at the savings alongside the fresh spinach and organic raspberries filling her basket. To the unassuming passer-by, the storefront is completely hidden, tucked away in an underground parking lot beneath The Ellery, a luxury apartment complex where the cheapest rent starts at nearly $5,000 (£3,725) a month. In fact, the building's own website completely omits the grocer from its curated online neighbourhood guide, choosing instead to highlight pricier nearby options like Whole Foods and Brooklyn Fare. But step past the luxury façade into the basement, and the quiet disappears. Even on an early Tuesday afternoon in July, the brightly lit, bustling space hums with high energy as a lunchtime crowd of New Yorkers tightly navigates the narrow aisles with oversized canvas bags.Porter's discovery is part of Aldi's $9bn US expansion plan to add 800 new stores over five years, specifically targeting dense urban hubs like Manhattan. It marks a massive scale-up for the German supermarket, which first entered the US in 1976 and has steadily grown its footprint to nearly 2,800 storefronts. The aggressive real estate blitz signals a bold shift for a brand traditionally associated with suburban strip malls and lower-end consumers. Image source, AldiImage caption, The Manhattan location is one of 800 new store openings Aldi has planned over five yearsIncumbent US grocers may look with some concern at the insurgency Aldi pulled off since it entered the UK market in the 1990s. Alongside fellow German supermarket Lidl, Aldi picked up huge swathes of the market by offering cheaper prices for high-quality goods. The traditional "big four" grocers at the time - Tesco, Sainsbury's, Asda and Morrisons - were slow to respond to the new competition, leaving the challengers to gradually pick off their shoppers.Today, Aldi is the UK's fourth biggest grocer, commanding 10.8% of the market.Its rapid growth is being mirrored across Europe, its rise aided by easing perceptions of it as a strictly lower cost grocer as shoppers became increasingly impressed by the quality of its products. The cost of living crisis of the 2020s further fuelled its ascent. However, while Aldi is rapidly ascending the ranks of American grocery consciousness, it is not, and may never aim to be, Walmart. Aldi currently holds just 2.9% of the US grocery pie, while Walmart controls about 20%.Yet analysts say that staying smaller is precisely how Aldi wins. Data from location analytics firm Placer.ai reveals Aldi is capturing middle- and higher-income shoppers with household incomes between $75,000 and $125,000. Years of persistent inflation have forced wealthier households to aggressively seek cheaper supermarkets."Those shoppers have started to trade off a visit to a conventional grocery store or a quick service restaurant and started to go into Aldi more frequently," RJ Hottovy, Placer.ai's head of analytical research, told the BBC. "They're looking for ways to stretch their household budget." For some urban commuters, the new city location offers a better experience than older formats. Kelvin Dozier, who usually shops at an Aldi in Brooklyn, recently started visiting the Manhattan location right across from his office for convenience. "The one here - it's brighter," Dozier told the BBC, noting the fresh sweet navel oranges in his basket. "The one in Brooklyn is a little smaller. It almost seems temporary, but here it looks like a permanent location." Still, winning over city slickers accustomed to premium brands remains an uphill battle. Ralph Montenegro, visiting Aldi for the first time, remained fiercely loyal to competitors. "It has more variety than say Target," Montenegro said, praising the prices on staples like flour and fruit, though he noted he still prefers Trader Joe's. He added that Aldi's heavy reliance on packaged, private-label processed foods was a detractor compared to the natural organic options he prefers. This strict reliance on limited, private labels is exactly what keeps Aldi's overheads low, according to Dustin York, an associate professor of communication at Maryville University. He says that Aldi targets a lean, highly efficient model that provides about 80% of what a traditional big-box retailer carries, but at a much lower cost. Still, York argues it is unlikely that Aldi will take dramatic market share from Walmart, because the retail giant is simply too massive. "I call Walmart the battleship, and I call Aldi a kind of submarine." But navigating those crowded waters can bring a distinct financial hazard. "Their biggest kryptonite is real estate cost," York warned, pointing to a brutal Manhattan retail landscape where average asking rents are between $350 and $700 per square foot.Image source, ALDIImage caption, Aldi hopes it can grow its share of the US grocery market in the same way it has in EuropeIn addition to high rents, Manhattan's roads provide another challenge.Speaking on Bloomberg's Odd Lots podcast, Aldi's US chief commercial officer Scott Patton detailed that supplying the Manhattan store requires trucking inventory in from South Windsor, Connecticut, using shorter, specialised trucks to navigate tight city streets. "We come at night because of the congestion," Patton said, noting that each truck requires a two-driver team to handle the city's turning radiuses. One driver watches for blind spots while the other unloads the groceries. To keep she
contentParagraphs.zh: 图片来源，Francisco Velasquez/BBC 图片说明，玛丽·波特 (Mary Porter) 首次在阿尔迪 (Aldi) 纽约曼哈顿新店购物 作者：弗朗西斯科·维拉斯奎兹 (Francisco Velasquez) 商业记者 当玛丽·波特 (Mary Porter) 走进曼哈顿最新的阿尔迪 (Aldi) 商店寻找便宜货时，这位长期居民发现了她眼中的零售奇迹：一罐 4 美元的杏仁酱，在她自己的社区售价 22 美元。 79 岁的波特对 BBC 表示：“阿尔迪以物美价廉着称，所以我想来看看，天哪，这真是太棒了。”她对篮子里装满的新鲜菠菜和有机覆盆子的省钱感到惊讶。对于不起眼的路人来说，店面是完全隐藏的，隐藏在豪华公寓大楼 The Ellery 下方的地下停车场中，最便宜的租金为每月近 5,000 美元（3,725 英镑）。事实上，该建筑自己的网站在其精心策划的在线社区指南中完全省略了杂货店，而是选择突出显示附近价格较高的选择，例如 Whole Foods 和 Brooklyn Fare。但越过豪华的外观进入地下室，安静就消失了。即使是在 7 月的周二下午早些时候，午餐时间的一群纽约人提着超大帆布袋紧紧地在狭窄的过道里穿行，灯火通明、熙熙攘攘的空间里也充满了活力。波特的发现是阿尔迪在美国耗资 90 亿美元的扩张计划的一部分，该计划将在五年内新增 800 家新店，特别针对曼哈顿等密集的城市中心。这标志着这家德国超市的大规模扩张，该超市于 1976 年首次进入美国，目前已拥有近 2,800 个店面。激进的房地产闪电战标志着这个传统上与郊区购物中心和低端消费者联系在一起的品牌的大胆转变。图片来源，Aldi 图片说明：曼哈顿店是 Aldi 计划在五年内开设的 800 家新店之一。自 20 世纪 90 年代进入英国市场以来，Aldi 所掀起的叛乱可能会让美国现有杂货商有些担忧。阿尔迪与德国超市利德尔 (Lidl) 一起，通过提供更便宜的高品质商品赢得了巨大的市场份额。当时的传统“四大”杂货商——特易购、塞恩斯伯里、阿斯达和莫里森——对新的竞争反应迟缓，使得挑战者逐渐淘汰了他们的购物者。如今，阿尔迪是英国第四大杂货商，占据了 10.8% 的市场份额。它的快速增长正在整个欧洲得到反映，随着购物者对商品质量越来越印象深刻，它的崛起得益于它作为严格低成本杂货商的看法。它的产品。 2020年代的生活成本危机进一步推动了其上涨。然而，虽然阿尔迪正在迅速提升美国食品杂货意识的排名，但它不是，也可能永远不会成为沃尔玛。阿尔迪目前仅占据美国食品杂货市场 2.9% 的份额，而沃尔玛则控制着约 20%。然而分析师表示，保持规模较小正是阿尔迪的获胜之道。位置分析公司 Placer.ai 的数据显示，Aldi 正在吸引家庭收入在 75,000 美元至 125,000 美元之间的中高收入购物者。多年持续的通货膨胀迫使富裕家庭积极寻找更便宜的超市。 Placer.ai 分析研究主管 RJ Hottovy 告诉 BBC：“这些购物者已经开始不再去传统杂货店或快餐店，而是开始更频繁地去阿尔迪。” “他们正在寻找扩大家庭预算的方法。”对于一些城市通勤者来说，新的城市位置比旧格式提供了更好的体验。凯尔文·多齐尔（Kelvin Dozier）通常在布鲁克林的阿尔迪（Aldi）购物，最近为了方便起见，他开始前往位于他办公室对面的曼哈顿店。 “这里的那个 - 它更明亮，”多齐尔告诉 BBC，他注意到篮子里有新鲜的甜脐橙。 “布鲁克林的那个要小一些。它看起来几乎是临时的，但在这里它看起来像是一个永久的地点。”尽管如此，赢得习惯于高端品牌的都市人的青睐仍然是一场艰苦的战斗。拉尔夫·蒙特内格罗（Ralph Montenegro）首次访问阿尔迪，仍然对竞争对手保持着强烈的忠诚。 Montenegro 说：“它的品种比 Target 还要多。”他对面粉和水果等主食的价格表示赞赏，但他指出自己仍然更喜欢 Trader Joe's。他补充说，与他喜欢的天然有机食品相比，阿尔迪对包装、自有品牌加工食品的严重依赖是一个不利因素。玛丽维尔大学传播学副教授达斯汀·约克表示，这种对有限的自有品牌的严格依赖正是阿尔迪保持较低管理费用的原因。他表示，阿尔迪的目标是建立一种精益、高效的模式，提供传统大型零售商约 80% 的商品，但成本却低得多。不过，约克认为阿尔迪不太可能从沃尔玛手中夺取巨大的市场份额，因为这家零售巨头规模太大了。 “我称沃尔玛为战舰，而我称阿尔迪为潜艇。”但在这些拥挤的水域航行可能会带来明显的财务风险。约克警告说：“他们最大的克星是房地产成本。”他指出，曼哈顿的零售业环境十分残酷，平均租金要价在每平方英尺 350 至 700 美元之间。图片来源，ALDI 图片说明，阿尔迪希望能够像在欧洲一样扩大其在美国食品杂货市场的份额。除了高租金之外，曼哈顿的道路也带来了另一个挑战。在彭博社的 Odd Lot 播客上，阿尔迪美国首席商务官 Scott Patton 详细介绍了这一点。为曼哈顿商店供货需要使用较短的专用卡车从康涅狄格州南温莎运来库存，在狭窄的城市街道上行驶。巴顿说：“由于交通拥堵，我们在晚上来。”他指出，每辆卡车都需要两名司机的团队来处理城市的转弯半径。一名司机留意盲点，另一名司机则负责卸货。为了留住她

articles.title: T. rex could become most expensive fossil ever - but it's a ​problem for scientists
articles.summary: A 67 million-year-old Tyrannosaurus rex will go on sale in New York with a pre-sale value of $30m.
articles.titleZh: 霸王龙可能成为有史以来最昂贵的化石 - 但这对科学家来说是一个问题
articles.summaryZh: 一只拥有 6700 万年历史的霸王龙将在纽约拍卖，预售价为 3000 万美元。

contentParagraphs.en: Image source, Matthew ShermanByEsme StallardSenior climate and science reporterIn 1997, Sotheby's hosted a natural history auction selling wonders of our prehistoric world - but for the first time this included a dinosaur.It was a niche event mostly attended by the world's museums looking for specimens to add to their collections.The dinosaur in question was a Tyrannosaurus Rex called Sue - she was eventually sold for $8m (£6m) to the Field Museum in Chicago.Nearly 30 years later, on Tuesday, another T. rex will make an appearance at the annual auction - one of the most complete specimens of this kind ever found.And this time it is not just scientists who are dinosaur-hunting but also the super-rich.The new specimen, known as Gus, has already been valued at $30m but it could fetch more, possibly even becoming the most expensive dinosaur ever sold.It adds to a growing debate in the natural history world – should specimens of such scientific importance be reserved for museums and their scientists?Or - as auctioneers would argue - should fossil hunters be rewarded for their discovery of dinosaurs lost to science and saving them from a second extinction?Cassandra Hatton, global head of natural history at Sotheby's, knows very well the lengths some fossil scientists - palaeontologists - are willing to go to in the search for these creatures."People die on excavations," she says.And for many of these hunters, the ultimate prize is the Tyrannosaurus Rex.This dinosaur that lived millions of years ago hardly needs describing, having been immortalised in our culture by appearances in films like King Kong and Jurassic Park, and as the namesake of an English rock band.Image source, Sotheby'sImage caption, Gus the T. rex was discovered in Badlands country in South Dakota and named after the late owner of the land"The people that look for these fossils will spend months out in the field with tents and their food in their backpacks and they're camping out in the middle of nowhere with the rattlesnakes and the bugs and the mountain lions," she explains.This is South Dakota - Badlands country - where Gus was eventually discovered 67 million years after roaming our planet.But finding it is almost the easy part, explains Dr Fiann Smithwick, an independent palaeontologist who has been collecting and preserving fossils for the past 20 years."Suddenly when they're out of the ground, they're out of equilibrium, and that normally means they start to decay, fall apart."Thomas Heitkamp and the team that discovered Gus - named after the late Gary "Gus" Licking, a cattle rancher whose land it was found on - spent three years carefully excavating."But it's not the full year," Cassandra Hatton explains. "You can only dig during the field season. So you have to wait till the ground has thawed. And then you are furiously digging until the ground freezes again [in September]."In 2023, the dig was complete, but the team was only halfway through the recovery process. They then spent a further three years documenting and reconstructing the T. rex back in the lab.Image source, Fiann SmithwickImage caption, Dr Fiann Smithwick explained how coming across the fossils is the start of a careful and ardous process of excavationTuesday's auction will be a payday that has been a long time coming for the team, and could be the biggest yet.Gus has the highest pre-sale valuation at $30m. The record for a dinosaur auction is currently held by Apex, a Stegosaurus sold by Sotheby's in 2024 for $44.6m - but that was 11 times what it had originally been valued at going into the auction.If you are inclined to put a bid in this time, your starting offer cannot be lower than $19m.For some of the oldest and largest museums in the world dealing in fossils, even this is out of reach."We're already priced out of having access to many, many specimens," explains Prof Susannah Maidment, dinosaur researcher at London's Natural History Museum (NHM).The five most expensive dinosaurs sold at auction have all been since 2020, including Stan a T. rex sold for $31.8m in 2020 - the guide price had been $6-8m. And this, she says, is "really problematic"."There's no substitute for having the real fossil. If we're going to do any sort of study, the number one thing is we need to understand the anatomy. We need to know what's real," Prof Maidment explains.And she said palaeobiology - the study of past life on the planet - has never been more important."We are in what is probably a mass extinction right now, we're changing our environment very, very, very rapidly. The past is really the only kind of empirical data we have to tell us about what is going on right now and in the future," she says.That is just the impact on the scientists. Prof Maidment says that for the public, being able to see the real bones of a dinosaur in a museum "helps them engage with the natural world".She says that dinosaur specimens are no longer being seen for their scientific value "but like we might view art" as something to collect by wealthy individuals, which has driven the price up.'A huge bite mark on the skull'Image source, Matthew ShermanImage caption, It took three years to fully excavate the fossils, and a further three years to document and reconstructCassandra Hatton argues that the price of Gus is a reflection of how important a specimen he is."Gus is one of the largest and most complete T. rex ever found, 61% of the bones has been identified - in general you find half of the skeleton that's a major scientific find," she says.The condition of the bones also provides deep insight into the kind of life this creature would have had."There's a huge bite mark on the top of the skull, which could have been sustained during a battle. You've got broken bones - some of the ribs, you see huge lumps where they broke and they healed."Cassandra Hatton says she has reached out to museums all over the world for months to get them to take part in the auction. She wants "something that's

articles.title: US Senator and close Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
articles.summary: The influential politician had just returned from Kyiv, where he met Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday.
articles.titleZh: 美国参议员兼特朗普亲密盟友林赛·格雷厄姆因“短暂而突然的疾病”去世
articles.summaryZh: 这位颇具影响力的政治家刚刚从基辅返回，周五他在那里会见了乌克兰总统弗拉基米尔·泽连斯基。

contentParagraphs.en: Republican Senator Lindsey Graham, a close ally of US President Donald Trump, has died at the age of 71.His office released a statement saying he died on Saturday evening following "a brief and sudden illness", adding that Graham's family had asked for "privacy during this incredibly difficult period". Elected to the Senate in 2002, the South Carolina politician was one of Washington's most influential voices on foreign policy. He had just returned from Kyiv, where he met the Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday. There were no known health concerns ahead of his trip.In a social media post marking the senator's death, US President Donald Trump said Graham was a "true American Patriot".Graham also served as Chairman of the Senate Budget Committee.He was a strong proponent of US support for Ukraine. In his most recent trip to the country, Graham was working on a version of the Russia sanctions bill, which he said would give "tools to President Trump to end this war". "Putin will not stop in Ukraine," he told the BBC in 2023. "To be weak in Ukraine means that you lose in Taiwan." Graham initially clashed with the US president. He told CNN in 2015 that Trump was "a race-baiting, xenophobic, religious bigot", and during the 2016 campaign he posted on social media: "If we nominate Trump, we will get destroyed... and we will deserve it."After the US Capitol riots in 2021, Graham gave a speech on the Senate floor in which he said: "Trump and I, we've had a hell of a journey. I hate it to end this way."All I can say is a count me out. Enough is enough."But he later became one of his staunchest backers. He voted against convicting Trump in the 2021 impeachment trial, and supported Trump in the 2024 election. He told the BBC in 2023: "There is a dark side to Donald Trump... and he was a very good president. But I am sticking with him because I saw what he did." He cited Trump's record on the US southern border, the killing of Iran's powerful military commander Qasem Soleimani and the appointment of conservative judges.Graham "consistently pushed for outcomes in the War on Terror that protect our long-term national security interests", his website said.He was opposed to the withdrawal of US troops from Afghanistan in 2021, describing it as a "sad and dangerous event for US national security". "Jihadists all over the world are celebrating," he added. "America will be seen as weak."Graham was also a staunch supporter of Israel. Israel's President Benjamin Netanyahu paid tribute to Graham on Sunday, saying "Lindsey understood that the security of Israel and America are inseparable". Israel lost "one of its greatest friends", he added.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国总统唐纳德·特朗普的亲密盟友、共和党参议员林赛·格雷厄姆去世，享年 71 岁。他的办公室发表声明称，林赛·格雷厄姆因“短暂而突然的疾病”于周六晚上去世，并补充说格雷厄姆的家人要求“在这个极其困难的时期保护隐私”。这位南卡罗来纳州政治家于 2002 年当选为参议员，是华盛顿在外交政策上最有影响力的声音之一。他刚刚从基辅回来，周五在那里会见了乌克兰总统泽连斯基。出行前没有已知的健康问题。美国总统唐纳德·特朗普在纪念这位参议员去世的社交媒体帖子中称格雷厄姆是“真正的美国爱国者”。格雷厄姆还担任参议院预算委员会主席。他是美国支持乌克兰的坚定支持者。在最近一次访问该国时，格雷厄姆正在制定俄罗斯制裁法案的一个版本，他表示该法案将为“特朗普总统结束这场战争提供工具”。 “普京不会在乌克兰停下来，”他在 2023 年告诉 BBC。“在乌克兰软弱意味着你在台湾失败。”格雷厄姆最初与美国总统发生冲突。 2015年，他告诉CNN，特朗普是“一个种族诱饵、仇外、宗教偏执者”，2016年竞选期间，他在社交媒体上发帖称：“如果我们提名特朗普，我们就会被摧毁……我们活该。”2021年美国国会大厦骚乱后，格雷厄姆在参议院发表演讲，他说：“特朗普和我，我们经历了一段地狱般的旅程。我讨厌结束这一切。”方式。”我只能说把我排除在外。够了。”但他后来成为了他最坚定的支持者之一。他在2021年弹劾审判中投票反对给特朗普定罪，并在2024年大选中支持特朗普。2023年他告诉BBC：“唐纳德·特朗普有阴暗面……但他是一位非常好的总统。但我坚持他，因为我看到了他的所作所为。”他引用了特朗普在美国南部边境的记录、伊朗强大的军事指挥官卡西姆·苏莱马尼被杀以及保守派法官的任命。他的网站称，格雷厄姆“一贯推动反恐战争取得成果，保护我们的长期国家安全利益”。他反对美国在2021年从阿富汗撤军，称这是“对美国国家安全来说令人悲伤和危险的事件”。 “世界各地的圣战分子都在庆祝，”他补充道，“美国将被视为软弱。”格雷厄姆也是以色列的坚定支持者，以色列总统本杰明·内塔尼亚胡周日向格雷厄姆表示敬意，他补充说，“林赛明白以色列和美国的安全是密不可分的”。

articles.title: Shy on the dance floor? Virtual reality 'partners' aim to help you find your groove
articles.summary: VR dance lesson apps like Dance Guru and Trip the Light offer a judgment-free way to learn partner dancing.
articles.titleZh: 在舞池里害羞？虚拟现实“合作伙伴”旨在帮助您找到最佳状态
articles.summaryZh: Dance Guru 和 Trip the Light 等 VR 舞蹈课程应用程序提供了一种无需判断的方式来学习舞伴舞蹈。

contentParagraphs.en: Entrepreneur David Huang tests out a VR headset while conducting demonstrations of the social dance lesson app Dance Guru at the Augmented World Expo in Long Beach, Calif., June 17, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 6 月 17 日，在加利福尼亚州长滩举行的增强世界博览会上，企业家 David Huang 在演示社交舞蹈课程应用 Dance Guru 时测试了 VR 耳机。

contentParagraphs.en: Wedding season is in full swing, bringing with it a familiar sense of dread for anyone who fears the dance floor.
contentParagraphs.zh: 婚礼季正如火如荼地进行，给那些害怕舞池的人带来一种熟悉的恐惧感。

contentParagraphs.en: But relief may finally be at hand with the help of a new app, Dance Guru, and a virtual reality (VR) headset.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但在新应用程序 Dance Guru 和虚拟现实 (VR) 耳机的帮助下，缓解可能终于来临。

contentParagraphs.en: The social dance instruction app transports users to a spacious, digital dance studio. Waiting inside is a computer-generated coach: a handsome, male avatar wearing a shirt open to his navel. He speaks with a slightly gravelly English accent.
contentParagraphs.zh: 社交舞蹈指导应用程序将用户带到宽敞的数字舞蹈工作室。里面等待着的是一位计算机生成的教练：一个英俊的男性化身，穿着一件开到肚脐的衬衫。他说话带有一点沙哑的英国口音。

contentParagraphs.en: "Watch me now," he instructs at the start of a waltz lesson — which NPR tried out at the Augmented World Expo in Long Beach, Calif., an annual conference showcasing the latest developments in virtual and augmented reality.
contentParagraphs.zh: “现在看我，”他在华尔兹课开始时说道，NPR 在加州长滩举行的增强世界博览会上尝试了这一课，这是一个展示虚拟和增强现实最新发展的年度会议。

contentParagraphs.en: The avatar then demonstrates a basic box step.
contentParagraphs.zh: 然后，化身演示了基本的拳击步骤。

contentParagraphs.en: From there, the lesson becomes interactive. The coach tells the user to hold his hand while an electric pinging sound tracks the student's foot placement.
contentParagraphs.zh: 从那时起，课程就变得互动了。教练告诉用户握住他的手，同时发出电击声来跟踪学生的脚部位置。

contentParagraphs.en: "One, two, three, four, five, six," the virtual teacher counts down.
contentParagraphs.zh: “一、二、三、四、五、六”，虚拟老师倒数。

contentParagraphs.en: When the user stumbles, he remains remarkably patient. "Do not worry, foundations take time. Let's try that again. Work on grounding your steps more intentionally."
contentParagraphs.zh: 当用户绊倒时，他仍然非常耐心。 “别担心，打基础需要时间。让我们再试一次。更有意识地为你的脚步奠定基础。”

contentParagraphs.en: Solving the beginner's dilemma
contentParagraphs.zh: 解决初学者的困境

contentParagraphs.en: Dance Guru creator David Huang said he came up with the idea for the app a couple of years ago out of frustration.
contentParagraphs.zh: Dance Guru 的创建者 David Huang 表示，他几年前出于沮丧而提出了这款应用程序的想法。

contentParagraphs.en: "I always wanted to learn to dance and I was always terrible at it," Huang said. "And I always ended up stopping midway through the lessons."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我一直想学跳舞，但我总是学得很差，”黄说。 “而且我总是在课程中途停下来。”

contentParagraphs.en: He soon realized that many beginners hit the exact same roadblocks.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他很快意识到许多初学者都遇到了完全相同的障碍。

contentParagraphs.en: "Private lessons are too expensive, and you feel like you're always forgetting the dance steps," Huang said. "You cannot find a partner to dance with. So I figured maybe I can create something like this."
contentParagraphs.zh: “私人课程太贵了，而且你感觉自己总是忘记舞步，”黄说。 “你找不到舞伴。所以我想也许我可以创造这样的东西。”

contentParagraphs.en: The Dance Guru platform currently offers tutorials in salsa, bachata, waltz, and cha-cha, in both lead and follow modes. To make the digital instruction feel authentic, Huang used motion-capture technology to record the movements of real-life dance teachers — with their permission.
contentParagraphs.zh: Dance Guru 平台目前提供萨尔萨舞、巴恰塔舞、华尔兹和恰恰舞的教程，包括引导模式和跟随模式。为了让数字教学感觉真实，黄在获得舞蹈老师许可的情况下，使用动作捕捉技术记录了现实生活中舞蹈老师的动作。

contentParagraphs.en: Building on the legacy of online tutorials and video games
contentParagraphs.zh: 建立在在线教程和视频游戏的基础上

contentParagraphs.en: Dance Guru belongs to a small but growing wave of apps using VR to demystify social dance. At a nearby booth, conference attendee Victor Chen is testing out a competing app called Trip the Light. It currently offers salsa lessons, as well as freestyle options, where a user can dance with a partner without having to learn specific steps.
contentParagraphs.zh: Dance Guru 属于一小批使用 VR 来揭开社交舞神秘面纱的应用程序，但规模虽小，但仍在不断增长。在附近的一个展位上，与会者 Victor Chen 正在测试一款名为 Trip the Light 的竞争应用程序。目前，它提供萨尔萨舞课程以及自由式选项，用户可以与舞伴一起跳舞，而无需学习特定的舞步。

contentParagraphs.en: Trip the Light's booth at the Augmented World Expo included posters of the app's virtual instructors. Real-life performers, who gave Trip the Light permission to motion capture their movements, were used as a basis for these avatars.
contentParagraphs.zh: Trip the Light 在增强世界博览会上的展位上贴有该应用程序虚拟教练的海报。这些化身的基础是现实生活中的表演者，他们允许 Trip the Light 捕捉他们的动作。

contentParagraphs.en: "A lot of times when you're trying to learn a choreography, it's watching a YouTube video and you have to pause it, rewind, and play it," Chen said. "If you were to have a virtual avatar dancing in front of you and correcting for any parts that you missed, it might be a lot easier."
contentParagraphs.zh: “很多时候，当你尝试学习编舞时，就是在观看 YouTube 视频，你必须暂停、倒带并播放它，”陈说。 “如果你有一个虚拟化身在你面前跳舞并纠正你错过的任何部分，这可能会容易得多。”

contentParagraphs.en: Interactive video games like Dance Dance Revolution and Just Dance, and YouTube tutorials have been helping people improve their skills in private for years. But those games are mostly aimed at solo players. Unlike the new generation of immersive VR apps, they cannot simulate the mechanics or confidence required for partner dancing on a live dance floor.
contentParagraphs.zh: 多年来，《热舞革命》和《舞力全开》等互动视频游戏以及 YouTube 教程一直在帮助人们私下提高技能。但这些游戏主要针对单人玩家。与新一代沉浸式 VR 应用程序不同，它们无法模拟在现场舞池上跳舞所需的技巧或信心。

contentParagraphs.en: The reality check
contentParagraphs.zh: 现实检验

contentParagraphs.en: But this kind of app won't work for every dancer.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但这种应用程序并不适合每个舞者。

contentParagraphs.en: "Everyone learns a little bit differently. And so unless you have a game that has lots of different ways of teaching, you're going to have things that work for some people and don't work for others," said Ariana Katana, a trained contemporary dancer and dance content creator who's active on YouTube, Twitch and other platforms. "Also, it's hard to dance with a headset on."
contentParagraphs.zh: “每个人的学习方式都有些不同。因此，除非你的游戏有多种不同的教学方式，否则你的游戏就会对某些人有效，而对另一些人则无效，”活跃在 YouTube、Twitch 和其他平台上的训练有素的当代舞者和舞蹈内容创作者 Ariana Katana 说道。 “而且，戴着耳机跳舞也很困难。”

contentParagraphs.en: And then there's the issue of not being able to physically feel a virtual partner's hand or shoulder while dancing with them. Patrick Ascolese, the creator of Trip the Light, said the experience could become more tactile in the future. "Haptic suits and wearables will be coming, but I think we're a little away from that," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 还有一个问题是，在与虚拟舞伴跳舞时，无法实际感受到虚拟舞伴的手或肩膀。 Trip the Light 的创始人帕特里克·阿斯科莱斯 (Patrick Ascolese) 表示，未来这种体验可能会变得更加触感。 “触觉套装和可穿戴设备将会出现，但我认为我们距离这一点还有点距离，”他说。

contentParagraphs.en: Ascolese said even with their limitations, immersive tools like Trip the Light have immense potential as judgment-free training grounds — giving reluctant dancers the baseline confidence they need to eventually step onto the dance floor with real partners in the real world, including at weddings.
contentParagraphs.zh: Ascolese 表示，尽管存在局限性，但像 Trip the Light 这样的沉浸式工具作为无判断力的训练场具有巨大的潜力，为不情愿的舞者提供了最终与现实世界中的真正伴侣一起踏上舞池所需的底线信心，包括在婚礼上。

contentParagraphs.en: "Just like anything else, practice makes perfect," said Ascolese. "So the more time you spend in VR with a virtual partner, it works towards helping you get over that social hurdle. We are teaching you the moves that you have to do in order to go out and have fun."
contentParagraphs.zh: “就像其他事情一样，熟能生巧，”阿斯科莱斯说。 “因此，你在虚拟现实中与虚拟伙伴一起度过的时间越多，它就能帮助你克服社交障碍。我们正在教你出去玩得开心时必须做的动作。”

contentParagraphs.en: Jennifer Vanasco edited the broadcast and digital versions of this story. Chloee Weiner mixed the audio.
contentParagraphs.zh: 詹妮弗·瓦纳斯科 (Jennifer Vanasco) 编辑了这个故事的广播版和数字版。克洛伊·韦纳 (Chloee Weiner) 混合了音频。

articles.title: Justice Department subpoenas New York Times reporters over Air Force One reporting
articles.summary: The Times says federal agents turned up on the doorsteps of several of its journalists to force grand jury testimony next week over their coverage of the Air Force One plane gifted to Trump by Qatar.
articles.titleZh: 司法部就空军一号的报道传唤《纽约时报》记者
articles.summaryZh: 《泰晤士报》称，联邦特工出现在几名记者家门口，迫使大陪审团下周就卡塔尔赠送给特朗普的空军一号飞机的报道出庭作证。

contentParagraphs.en: The New York Times says federal agents showed up at several of its journalists' homes Friday night to try to force them to testify before a grand jury next week.
contentParagraphs.zh: 《纽约时报》称，联邦特工周五晚上出现在几名记者家中，试图迫使他们下周在大陪审团面前作证。

contentParagraphs.en: Michael M. Santiago/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 迈克尔·M·圣地亚哥/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Michael M. Santiago/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 迈克尔·M·圣地亚哥/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: The New York Times says several of its journalists have been subpoenaed by the Department of Justice over their reporting on Air Force One, describing it as a "brazen act."
contentParagraphs.zh: 《纽约时报》称，其几名记者因报道空军一号而受到司法部传唤，称这是“无耻的行为”。

contentParagraphs.en: On Wednesday, the newspaper published an anonymously sourced story that the Secret Service urged President Trump to leave the recent NATO summit in Turkey on an older version of Air Force One instead of the Boeing 747 donated by Qatar last year because of security concerns. The following day, the Times reported, again citing anonymous sources, that the gifted plane lacked "defensive countermeasures that were security features of the old model, including its advanced antimissile capabilities."
contentParagraphs.zh: 周三，该报发表了一篇匿名报道，称出于安全考虑，特勤局敦促特朗普总统乘坐旧版空军一号而不是卡塔尔去年捐赠的波音 747 离开最近在土耳其举行的北约峰会。第二天，《泰晤士报》再次援引匿名消息人士的话说，这架天才飞机缺乏“旧型号安全功能的防御对策，包括先进的反导弹能力”。

contentParagraphs.en: The four reporters bylined on Wednesday's article — Julian E. Barnes, Eric Lipton, Tyler Pager and Eric Schmitt — all received subpoenas, according to the Times. The paper said federal agents delivered the subpoenas Friday evening to some reporters at their homes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据《泰晤士报》报道，周三文章中署名的四名记者——朱利安·E·巴恩斯、埃里克·利普顿、泰勒·佩格和埃里克·施密特——都收到了传票。该报称，联邦特工周五晚上向一些在家中的记者发出了传票。

contentParagraphs.en: The subpoenas "seek to force the reporters to testify before a federal grand jury in Manhattan on Wednesday," the Times reported. Their testimony, according to the subpoenas, was requested "in regard to an alleged violation of federal criminal law."
contentParagraphs.zh: 据《泰晤士报》报道，这些传票“旨在迫使记者周三在曼哈顿的联邦大陪审团面前作证”。根据传票，他们的证词是“因涉嫌违反联邦刑法”而被要求的。

contentParagraphs.en: "The appearance of Federal law enforcement agents on the doorstep of news reporters should shock the conscience of any American who believes in the Constitution and the press freedom it protects," David McCraw, senior vice president and deputy general counsel for the Times, said in a statement. "Our journalists report the facts and advance the American public's right to know how their government is operating and their taxpayer dollars are being used. This brazen act should be seen as nothing more than an attempt to prevent the public from knowing what is happening in their country by intimidating journalists from doing their jobs."
contentParagraphs.zh: 《纽约时报》高级副总裁兼副总法律顾问大卫·麦克劳在一份声明中表示：“联邦执法人员出现在新闻记者家门口，应该会震惊任何相信宪法及其保护的新闻自由的美国人的良心。” “我们的记者报道事实，并促进美国公众了解政府如何运作以及纳税人的钱如何使用的权利。这种厚颜无耻的行为应该被视为试图通过恐吓记者履行职责来阻止公众了解他们国家正在发生的事情。”

contentParagraphs.en: Before the Times published the Wednesday article, a senior FBI official had contacted a reporter and editor and asked that the story be held, without explaining why, a New York Times spokesman tells NPR. The FBI official also asked that the sources for the story be identified. Both Times employees refused to do either. (The Times itself was first to report an account of these events.)
contentParagraphs.zh: 《纽约时报》发言人告诉美国公共广播电台，在《纽约时报》发表周三的文章之前，一名联邦调查局高级官员联系了一名记者和编辑，要求保留这篇报道，但没有解释原因。联邦调查局官员还要求查明该报道的来源。两家《纽约时报》的员工都拒绝这样做。 （《泰晤士报》本身首先报道了这些事件。）

contentParagraphs.en: The subpoenas were issued by U.S. Attorney Jay Clayton of the Southern District of New York, who was recently nominated by Trump to be the next national intelligence director. Both the White House and the FBI declined to comment for this story. NPR reached out to the Southern District of New York but did not hear back.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这些传票是由纽约南区美国检察官杰伊·克莱顿发出的，他最近被特朗普提名为下一任国家情报总监。白宫和联邦调查局均拒绝对此事发表评论。美国国家公共广播电台联系了纽约南区，但没有收到回复。

contentParagraphs.en: The president of the Reporters Committee for Freedom of the Press, Bruce D. Brown, said in a statement Saturday, "The subpoenas ... issued to journalists at The New York Times break from longstanding Justice Department practice to protect the public interest and press independence by requiring prosecutors to only seek information from reporters as a last resort when all other avenues have been exhausted. When Jay Clayton appears before the Senate Intelligence Committee on Wednesday, members of both parties must not let him escape accountability."
contentParagraphs.zh: 新闻自由记者委员会主席布鲁斯·D·布朗在周六的一份声明中表示，“向《纽约时报》记者发出的传票……违反了司法部长期以来保护公共利益和新闻独立的惯例，要求检察官只有在所有其他途径都已用尽的情况下才将向记者寻求信息作为最后手段。当杰伊·克莱顿周三出现在参议院情报委员会时，两党成员都不能让他逃避责任。”

contentParagraphs.en: The move to subpoena the Times journalists is the latest escalation in Trump's years-long effort to cow and control U.S. media outlets, following previous financial settlements with ABC News and CBS News' 60 Minutes program, alongside civil lawsuits and federal criminal actions taken against The Wall Street Journal, The Washington Post, the BBC and others since he resumed office last year.
contentParagraphs.zh: 传唤《泰晤士报》记者的举动是特朗普多年来恐吓和控制美国媒体的最新升级举措，此前特朗普曾与美国广播公司新闻频道和哥伦比亚广播公司新闻频道的“60分钟”节目达成财务和解，并自去年复任以来对《华尔街日报》、《华盛顿邮报》、BBC等媒体提起民事诉讼和联邦刑事诉讼。

contentParagraphs.en: In an unusual step earlier this year, the FBI searched the property of Washington Post reporter Hannah Natanson, seizing her phones and laptops, as part of an investigation into leaked information. Natanson had written a series of in-depth stories about the Trump administration's attempts to reduce the federal workforce.
contentParagraphs.zh: 今年早些时候，联邦调查局采取了一个不同寻常的举措，搜查了《华盛顿邮报》记者汉娜·纳坦森的财产，没收了她的手机和笔记本电脑，作为对泄露信息的调查的一部分。纳坦森撰写了一系列有关特朗普政府试图减少联邦劳动力的深入报道。

contentParagraphs.en: Trump is currently embroiled in several simultaneous personal legal disputes with the Times over its coverage of him. He has accused the publication of disparaging his reputation, undermining his efforts to win reelection and defamation. The newspaper has rejected his claims.
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普目前因《纽约时报》对他的报道而与《纽约时报》同时卷入几起个人法律纠纷。他指责该出版物贬低了他的声誉，破坏了他赢得连任和诽谤的努力。该报否认了他的说法。

contentParagraphs.en: The Times has also launched its own legal action against the Defense Department for seeking to restrict Pentagon access to reporters, and the paper is involved in a separate claim and counterclaim with the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission. The commission accuses the paper of discriminatory employment practices based on a complaint filed by a white male editor who said he had been passed over for a promotion, while the Times said the commission's lawsuit was part of the Trump administration's retaliation for its coverage of the president.
contentParagraphs.zh: 《纽约时报》还针对国防部寻求限制五角大楼接触记者的行为发起了法律诉讼，该报还向美国平等就业机会委员会提出了单独的索赔和反索赔。该委员会根据一名白人男性编辑提出的投诉，指控该报存在歧视性就业做法，该编辑称自己没有获得晋升，而《泰晤士报》则表示，该委员会的诉讼是特朗普政府对其报道总统的报复的一部分。

articles.title: RSPB buys 'magical' 96-hectare landscape to reconnect habitats
articles.summary: The move will help conservationists in Gallt-y-bere to protect internationally important habitats.
articles.titleZh: RSPB 购买“神奇”的 96 公顷景观以重新连接栖息地
articles.summaryZh: 此举将帮助高尔特贝雷的自然资源保护者保护国际重要的栖息地。

contentParagraphs.en: The reserve is now large enough to support breeding hen harriers, with the "red-listed" bird seen occasionally in the area but not recently attempting to breed, the charity said.It has been active at Gwenffrwd-Dinas for more than a century, first by supporting the Kite Committee in their efforts to protect red kites, and later by acquiring land to protect vulnerable habitats and wildlife.With Gallt-y-bere now protected, RSPB Cymru said it was taking another important step towards creating a more resilient future for Wales' wildlife.Surveys over the summer, funded by Natural Resources Wales, will help identify the full range of habitats and species present across the newly acquired land and inform future management.Existing wildlife-rich grasslands may be expanded and enhanced, providing greater resilience for important plants and wildlife, including species such as whorled caraway and wood bitter-vetch, the RSPB said.The charity added that it would also create opportunities to work closely with local farming tenants to demonstrate how nature recovery and sustainable farming can successfully work hand in hand across the wider landscape.In the future, RSPB Cymru said it planned to work with local communities and partners to restore and protect the newly connected landscape, ensuring it supports wildlife for generations to come.

articles.title: An artist brought 'I.C.E. pops' to a Texas campus. The show was shut down in days
articles.summary: The Trump administration's executive orders have meant that administrators are questioning what art can — and can't — be seen on campus.
articles.titleZh: 一位艺术家带来了“I.C.E.”流行到德克萨斯州的一个校园。该剧几天后就被停播了
articles.summaryZh: 特朗普政府的行政命令意味着管理人员正在质疑什么艺术可以在校园里看到，什么不能在校园里看到。

contentParagraphs.en: Victor "Marka27" Quiñonez, the Mexican-born, Texas-raised visual artist in his studio in Ridgewood, Queens, on May 14, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 5 月 14 日，墨西哥出生、得克萨斯州长大的视觉艺术家 Victor“Marka27”Quiñonez 在他位于皇后区里奇伍德的工作室里。

contentParagraphs.en: Laila AnnMarie Stevens for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的莱拉·安玛丽·史蒂文斯

contentParagraphs.en: Laila AnnMarie Stevens for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的莱拉·安玛丽·史蒂文斯

contentParagraphs.en: An art exhibition by Victor "Marka27" Quiñonez, whose work comments on issues facing U.S. Latinos, seemed like a natural fit for the campus of the University of North Texas in Denton, north of Dallas. Nearly a quarter of the students are Hispanic and the show focused on Quiñonez's dual cultural identity, as someone born in Ciudad Juárez, Mexico, and raised in East Dallas, Texas.
contentParagraphs.zh: 维克多·“Marka27”·奎尼奥内斯的艺术展似乎很适合位于达拉斯北部丹顿的北德克萨斯大学校园，他的作品评论了美国拉丁裔面临的问题。近四分之一的学生是西班牙裔，该节目重点关注奎诺内斯的双重文化身份，他出生于墨西哥华雷斯城，在德克萨斯州达拉斯东部长大。

contentParagraphs.en: Before it traveled to Texas, the show, called called "Ni De Aquí, Ni De Allá — Neither from Here Nor from There," had a successful run in Massachusetts at Boston University. But within a couple of days of its opening at UNT on Feb. 3, Quiñonez was being pinged on social media by students: The exhibition had been shut down.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在前往德克萨斯州之前，这个名为“Ni De Aquí，Ni De Allá——既不来自这里也不来自那里”的节目在马萨诸塞州的波士顿大学成功举办。但 2 月 3 日在 UNT 开幕后的几天内，Quiñonez 就被学生们在社交媒体上留言：展览已被关闭。

contentParagraphs.en: "Ni De Aquí, Ni De Allá" incorporated pieces from a series Quiñonez calls "I.C.E. Scream," which includes large resin sculptures in bright colors.
contentParagraphs.zh: “Ni De Aqui, Ni De Allá”融合了 Quiñonez 称之为“I.C.E. Scream”系列的作品，其中包括色彩鲜艳的大型树脂雕塑。

contentParagraphs.en: "They're in the shape of a Mexican paleta, which is a Mexican popsicle that you would see the paleteros pushing around in their little carts," the artist said in a recent interview at his studio in Queens, New York.
contentParagraphs.zh: “它们的形状像墨西哥帕莱塔，这是一种墨西哥冰棒，你会看到帕莱塔人在他们的小推车里推来推去，”这位艺术家最近在纽约皇后区的工作室接受采访时说道。

contentParagraphs.en: At first glance, those brightly-colored treats look like fun, nostalgic pop art. But there's something else going on, too. Each one has an item encapsulated inside of it, including handcuffs, replica firearms and old rosaries.
contentParagraphs.zh: 乍一看，这些色彩鲜艳的食物看起来就像有趣、怀旧的波普艺术。但还有其他事情正在发生。每一个里面都封装了一件物品，包括手铐、仿制枪械和旧念珠。

contentParagraphs.en: "When you're a child finishing your ice cream, there was sometimes a stamp or a joke that was put onto the popsicle stick," Quiñonez said. But what's stamped on these sticks is not a corny Dad joke, but a parody of the official U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement seal with text that reads: "U.S. Inhumane and Cruelty Enforcement."
contentParagraphs.zh: “当你还是个孩子吃完冰淇淋时，有时会在冰棒棒上贴上邮票或笑话，”奎尼奥内斯说。但这些棍子上印的并不是老套的老爸笑话，而是对美国移民和海关执法局官方印章的模仿，上面写着：“美国不人道和残忍执法。”

contentParagraphs.en: Victor "Marka27" Quiñonez holds portions of his sculpture from his "I.C.E. Scream" series in his studio.
contentParagraphs.zh: Victor“Marka27”Quiñonez 在他的工作室里保存着他的“I.C.E. Scream”系列雕塑的一部分。

contentParagraphs.en: Laila AnnMarie Stevens for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的莱拉·安玛丽·史蒂文斯

contentParagraphs.en: Laila AnnMarie Stevens for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的莱拉·安玛丽·史蒂文斯

contentParagraphs.en: Victor "Marka27" Quiñonez, Mexican-born, Brooklyn-based visual artist holds the acrylic sculpture "Drippy Paleta Lime / Uva flavor" from his "I.C.E. Scream" series at his studio in Ridgewood, Queens.
contentParagraphs.zh: Victor “Marka27” Quiñonez 是出生于墨西哥、现居布鲁克林的视觉艺术家，他在皇后区里奇伍德的工作室里拿着他的“I.C.E. Scream”系列中的亚克力雕塑“Drippy Paleta Lime / Uvaflavor”。

contentParagraphs.en: Laila AnnMarie Stevens for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的莱拉·安玛丽·史蒂文斯

contentParagraphs.en: Laila AnnMarie Stevens for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的莱拉·安玛丽·史蒂文斯

contentParagraphs.en: Quiñonez' work has been presented around the country, including at The Shed, a cultural center in New York City. Shed curator Deja Belardo said people see themselves reflected in Quiñonez's art.
contentParagraphs.zh: Quiñonez 的作品曾在全国各地展出，其中包括在纽约市的文化中心 The Shed。 Shed 策展人 Deja Belardo 表示，人们在 Quiñonez 的艺术中看到了自己的影子。

contentParagraphs.en: "We immediately saw the value of Victor's work and being responsive to what was happening around us," Belardo said. "The way he was wanting to tell a story about immigrants and their labor, and all of the work that they do in the shadows."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们立即看到了维克多工作的价值以及对我们周围发生的事情做出的反应，”贝拉多说。 “他想要讲述一个关于移民和他们的劳动，以及他们在暗处所做的所有工作的故事。”

contentParagraphs.en: Managing 'barking' from Austin
contentParagraphs.zh: 管理奥斯汀的“吠叫”

contentParagraphs.en: The University of North Texas did not respond to multiple requests from NPR for comment on why they had closed the exhibition. But an independent arts journalist, Adam Schrader, the founder of the site Urgent Matter, filed a public records access request. He obtained text messages between UNT President Harrison Keller and then-Provost Michael McPherson about the show's removal, and posted some of them online. McPherson stepped down from the position in May to become a senior advisor to Keller.
contentParagraphs.zh: 北德克萨斯大学没有回应 NPR 就其关闭展览原因置评的多次请求。但独立艺术记者、Urgent Matter 网站的创始人 Adam Schrader 提出了公共记录访问请求。他获得了北卡罗来纳大学校长哈里森·凯勒和时任教务长迈克尔·麦克弗森之间关于该节目被删除的短信，并将其中一些发布到网上。麦克弗森于五月辞去职务，成为凯勒的高级顾问。

contentParagraphs.en: Initially, the administrators discussed removing some of the pieces from the show. But then the provost texted that he wanted to take down the entire exhibition instead. McPherson wrote, "I think it'll be easier to manage any barking from our friends in Austin."
contentParagraphs.zh: 最初，管理员讨论了从演出中删除一些作品。但随后教务长发短信说他想取消整个展览。麦克弗森写道：“我认为处理奥斯汀朋友的吠叫会更容易。”

contentParagraphs.en: Austin is the home of Texas' state legislature. Schrader pointed out that this situation at UNT reflects a larger trend across the U.S. of universities reversing diversity, equity and inclusion efforts that they formerly championed.
contentParagraphs.zh: 奥斯汀是德克萨斯州立法机关的所在地。施拉德指出，UNT 的这种情况反映了整个美国大学的一个更大趋势，即逆转他们以前倡导的多样性、公平和包容性努力。

contentParagraphs.en: Victor "Marka27" Quiñonez's crafts the resin portion of paleta sculptures from his "I.C.E. Scream" series.
contentParagraphs.zh: Victor“Marka27”Quiñonez 制作了他的“I.C.E. Scream”系列帕莱塔雕塑的树脂部分。

contentParagraphs.en: Laila AnnMarie Stevens for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的莱拉·安玛丽·史蒂文斯

contentParagraphs.en: Laila AnnMarie Stevens for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的莱拉·安玛丽·史蒂文斯

contentParagraphs.en: "It comes after a lot of discourse politically around diversity, equity and inclusion at universities and just free speech in general at universities, art displays, at campuses from Florida to Texas and across the nation," Schrader said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 施拉德说：“这是在大学里围绕多样性、公平和包容性进行了大量政治讨论之后，在大学、艺术展览、从佛罗里达州到德克萨斯州以及全国各地的校园里普遍存在言论自由。”

contentParagraphs.en: NPR and NPR member stations have previously reported on the University of North Carolina board of governors voting to repeal the school's DEI policy in 2024; last year, lawmakers in Ohio introduced measures to limit or eliminate DEI initiatives at all public universities in their state.
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR和NPR成员台此前曾报道过北卡罗来纳大学董事会投票决定于2024年废除该校的DEI政策；去年，俄亥俄州立法者采取措施限制或取消该州所有公立大学的 DEI 举措。

contentParagraphs.en: "It's not like they were directly censored," Schrader said of UNT. "There were no politicians that directly came and said, 'You cannot have this show.'" Schrader said that's where the real story is.
contentParagraphs.zh: “他们并没有受到直接审查，”施拉德谈到 UNT 时说道。 “没有政客直接过来说，‘你不能举办这个节目。’”施拉德说，这才是真正的故事。

contentParagraphs.en: The White House has withheld billions from federally-funded universities whose diversity and inclusion policies do not align with those of President Trump. This is similar to policies enacted in some state legislatures around the country. In 2023, Texas began abolishing DEI programs at state-funded colleges and universities — including UNT — via a state senate bill.
contentParagraphs.zh: 白宫扣留了联邦资助大学的数十亿美元，这些大学的多样性和包容性政策与特朗普总统的政策不一致。这与全国一些州立法机构制定的政策类似。 2023 年，德克萨斯州开始通过州参议院法案废除国家资助的学院和大学（包括 UNT）的 DEI 项目。

contentParagraphs.en: One of the UNT students who reached out to alert Quiñonez about his exhibition being shuttered was Jenny Yanez, who graduated from the school in May.
contentParagraphs.zh: 珍妮·亚内兹 (Jenny Yanez) 是一名 UNT 学生，她于 5 月份从该校毕业，她向 Quiñonez 发出了关于他的展览被关闭的警告。

contentParagraphs.en: In an interview shortly before her graduation, Yanez said the mood within the arts program definitely shifted during her last few months at the university.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在毕业前不久的一次采访中，亚涅斯表示，在她在大学的最后几个月里，艺术项目中的情绪肯定发生了变化。

contentParagraphs.en: "I feel like everyone is acting like they're walking on eggshells and they feel very nervous as artists," she said. For example, members of the UNT arts faculty wrote an open letter in protest over the exhibition's closure — but did not sign their names.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我觉得每个人都表现得如履薄冰，作为艺术家他们感到非常紧张，”她说。例如，UNT 艺术学院的成员写了一封公开信，抗议展览的关闭，但没有署名。

contentParagraphs.en: An installation styled as a paleta cart is part of Victor "Marka27" Quiñonez's "I.C.E. Scream" series.
contentParagraphs.zh: 帕莱塔车风格的装置是 Victor“Marka27”Quiñonez 的“I.C.E. Scream”系列的一部分。

contentParagraphs.en: Laila AnnMarie Stevens
contentParagraphs.zh: 莱拉·安玛丽·史蒂文斯

contentParagraphs.en: Laila AnnMarie Stevens
contentParagraphs.zh: 莱拉·安玛丽·史蒂文斯

contentParagraphs.en: Yanez said there's been a lot of anger among students — especially around what she says has been a lack o
contentParagraphs.zh: 亚涅斯说，学生们非常愤怒，尤其是她所说的缺乏学习能力。

articles.title: Meta pulls new AI image feature after days of backlash
articles.summary: Meta's release this week of an AI feature that let people alter Instagram content drew swift blowback.
articles.titleZh: 经过几天的强烈反对后，Meta 取消了新的人工智能图像功能
articles.summaryZh: Meta 本周发布了一项人工智能功能，可以让人们改变 Instagram 内容，但很快就引起了反弹。

contentParagraphs.en: Meta has abruptly taken down a new feature that allowed people to use its artificial intelligence (AI) tool to make fake images from user content on Instagram. The feature was part of a broad rollout of Muse Image, a new AI image generation tool Instagram's parent company released on Tuesday.It allowed users of the Meta AI chatbot to tag public-facing accounts on Instagram and quickly use content on those accounts to create AI-generated or altered content and images. The feature quickly sparked blowback due to privacy concerns, leading Meta to admit it had “missed the mark" so it was "no longer available”.Muse Image was the tech firm's first foray into AI image generation but faced backlash as Instagram users were opted in by default. It meant that anyone with a public account could have their likeness used without their knowledge or permission. Hollywood union Sag-Aftra described the U-turn as a "win". It had previously urged its members and "all Instagram users" to take action to protect their likeness stating that there had been an "utter miscalculation of public sentiment regarding the obvious dangers and harms inherent in such use".The London-based human rights charity Privacy International had also criticised the feature, telling the BBC it was "the latest sign AI companies see people's images and data as raw material to be exploited".“Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way,” Meta added in its decision to pull the feature. “We've heard the feedback.”When Meta announced Muse Image, external, the firm said it was limited to Instagram, but more AI features and integrations were planned for WhatsApp, Facebook and Messenger.It also has an AI video tool in development.Meta declined to make any further comment.
contentParagraphs.zh: Meta 突然取消了一项新功能，该功能允许人们使用其人工智能 (AI) 工具根据 Instagram 上的用户内容制作虚假图像。该功能是 Instagram 母公司周二发布的新型 AI 图像生成工具 Muse Image 广泛推出的一部分。它允许 Meta AI 聊天机器人的用户标记 Instagram 上面向公众的帐户，并快速使用这些帐户上的内容来创建 AI 生成或更改的内容和图像。由于隐私问题，该功能很快引发了反弹，Meta 承认它“没有达到目标”，因此“不再可用”。Muse Image 是这家科技公司首次涉足人工智能图像生成领域，但由于 Instagram 用户默认选择加入，因此面临强烈反对。这意味着任何拥有公共账户的人都可以在未经其知情或许可的情况下使用其肖像。好莱坞工会 Sag-Aftra 将这一转变描述为“胜利”。该组织此前曾敦促其会员和“所有 Instagram 用户”采取行动保护自己的肖像，并表示“对于此类使用所固有的明显危险和危害，公众情绪完全误判”。总部位于伦敦的人权慈善机构“隐私国际”也批评了这一功能，告诉 BBC，这是“人工智能公司将人们的图像和数据视为可利用的原材料的最新迹象”。 Meta 在决定取消该功能时补充道。 “我们已经听到了反馈。” 当 Meta 对外宣布 Muse Image 时，该公司表示它仅限于 Instagram，但计划为 WhatsApp、Facebook 和 Messenger 提供更多 AI 功能和集成。它还有一个 AI 视频工具正在开发中。Meta 拒绝发表任何进一步评论。

articles.title: Reeves tells BBC: Burnham needs worked-through plan to govern from the start
articles.summary: The Chancellor tells Laura Kuenssberg she will be handing over a stable economy to the incoming PM.
articles.titleZh: 里夫斯告诉 BBC：伯纳姆需要从一开始就制定完善的治理计划
articles.summaryZh: 德国总理告诉劳拉·昆斯伯格，她将把稳定的经济交给即将上任的总理。

contentParagraphs.en: We sat down in one of the lavish 17th Century state rooms upstairs in No 11 Downing Street - exactly the same room where she gave her first full interview as chancellor in July 2024.She would never have suspected then that she and her next-door neighbour would be moving out just 24 months later. Reeves wouldn't explicitly say who should be the next chancellor, or even if she would like to stay. She has always told us that being chancellor is her "dream job". She and her team clearly do not expect to stay in No 11, but with the incoming No 10 team tight-lipped about its cast list, we just don't know.Reeves said that she had returned "stability and trust" to the economy over the past two years, and that "Andy will take over an economy that is much stronger than the one I inherited from the Conservatives just two years ago."In the interview, Reeves wanted to focus on what she described as the "big picture" - government borrowing costs that have gone down, inflation way down from its peak, increased investment in infrastructure like roads and railways, and the economy growing faster than the UK's nearest competitors.But by other measures, there are still big problems in the economy. Inflation is still above target and is expected to rise, growth has been slow, and just this week, the Bank of England warned that interest rates might have to go up again.The country's debts are due to be higher at the end of this parliament than they were when Labour moved in. And more than anything else, firms and families' spending power is still under pressure, with the latest ONS figures showing disposable income falling.One former senior minister told me Reeves had "spent a lot of time and energy painting a picture of her grim inheritance in the expectation things would brighten up quickly and she could claim credit"."By the time she found that things were different, with tight public finances, mushrooming welfare, and the economy stuck in a low-growth trap, she had used up all her political capital and was unable to win the big arguments on welfare reform," they said."It's all actually sad because I think her instincts are generally OK, but by the time she figured out the right things to do, it was too late".A close political ally of Starmer, Reeves became shadow chancellor in 2021 as Labour campaigned to return to power, resulting in their landslide victory two years ago.Many Labour MPs believe mistakes made in No 11 soured the prospects for Starmer's government soon after he and Reeves moved into Downing Street. Starmer himself has pointed to the decision to take away the winter fuel allowance from millions of pensioners, later partly reversed, as being one of the government's errors.A senior Labour figure told me: "She'd underestimated the desire for radical change, and lacked political nous on key decisions like winter fuel."Reeves however, would not acknowledge this had been a specific problem, and was eager to highlight the progress she believed the economy has made on her watch. Nor was she in the mood to admit that her relationship with business had worsened after she hiked National Insurance tax for employers.One City source told me the increasing costs for business had an immediate effect, and led to them laying off staff. "There was so much goodwill, but it was genuinely staggering - it just went in a few weeks," they said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 我们在唐宁街 11 号楼上一间豪华的 17 世纪国事厅里坐下来，这正是她 2024 年 7 月作为财政大臣首次接受全面面试的房间。当时她绝不会想到，仅仅 24 个月后，她和她的隔壁邻居就会搬出去。里夫斯没有明确表示谁应该成为下一任财政大臣，也没有明确表示她是否愿意留下来。她总是告诉我们，担任总理是她的“梦想工作”。她和她的团队显然不希望留在第11位，但即将上任的第10位团队对演员名单守口如瓶，我们只是不知道。里夫斯表示，过去两年她已经让经济恢复了“稳定和信任”，并且“安迪将接手一个比我两年前从保守党继承的经济要强大得多的经济。”在采访中，里夫斯想把重点放在她所说的“大局”上——政府借贷成本下降，通胀从峰值大幅回落，公路和铁路等基础设施投资增加，经济增长速度超过英国最接近的竞争对手。但从其他衡量标准来看，经济仍然存在很大问题。通货膨胀仍然高于目标，并且预计会上升，经济增长一直缓慢，就在本周，英国央行警告称，利率可能不得不再次上升。在本届议会结束时，英国的债务将比工党上台时更高。最重要的是，企业和家庭的消费能力仍然面临压力，最新的国家统计局数据显示可支配收入下降。一位前高级部长告诉我，里夫斯“花了很多时间和精力描绘了她严峻的情况”。他们说：“当她发现情况有所不同时，公共财政紧缩，福利激增，经济陷入低增长陷阱，她已经用尽了所有政治资本，无法赢得福利改革的重大争论。这一切实际上都很悲伤，因为我认为她的直觉总体上还不错，但当她想出正确的做法时，已经太晚了。”斯塔默和里夫斯于 2021 年成为影子财政大臣，工党在竞选中重新掌权，并在两年前取得了压倒性胜利。许多工党议员认为，在斯塔默和里夫斯入主唐宁街后不久，11 号法案中犯下的错误就让斯塔默政府的前景黯然失色。斯塔默本人指出，取消数百万养老金领取者冬季燃料补贴的决定是政府的错误之一，后来部分撤销。一位高级工党人士告诉我：“她低估了彻底变革的愿望，并且在冬季燃料等关键决策上缺乏政治理智。”然而，里夫斯不承认这是一个具体问题，并急于强调她认为在她领导下经济取得的进展。她也没有心情承认，在提高雇主的国民保险税后，她与企业的关系恶化了。一位市政府消息人士告诉我，企业成本的增加产生了立竿见影的影响，并导致他们裁员。他们说：“人们有如此多的善意，但这确实令人震惊——几周后就消失了。”

articles.title: Heatwave to stretch into second week despite cooler air for some this weekend
articles.summary: The sizzling heat continues into next week despite a drop in temperatures over the weekend.
articles.titleZh: 尽管本周末空气较凉爽，但热浪仍将持续到第二周
articles.summaryZh: 尽管周末气温有所下降，但炎热的天气仍将持续到下周。

contentParagraphs.en: While heatwave conditions will continue across many parts of England and Wales, a brisker wind means temperatures will still drop relative to recent days.North-easterly winds mean areas experiencing the biggest drop in temperature will be those close to North Sea coasts, with highs on Sunday and Monday around 18-21C.Even with the breeze it remains hot across the western half of England and Wales with highs of 26-30C widely, but 31-33C possible in parts of south-east Wales and south-west England.The high temperatures and dry conditions coupled with freshening winds will result in extreme wildfire conditions for many parts of England and Wales through the weekend, the Met Office warned.A National Fire Chiefs Council spokesperson said: "When the weather stays hot and dry, it only takes one spark to start a wildfire. What can begin as a small fire can spread incredibly quickly, putting people, homes, wildlife and our countryside at risk."By Sunday, parts of Scotland and Northern Ireland will see more sunshine as temperatures rise slightly. Parts of western Northern Ireland could reach or exceed the 25C heatwave threshold temperature.
