articles.title: Getting campaign text messages ahead of midterms? There could be an AI bot behind it
articles.summary: Taught to sound like a candidate, bots are engaging voters with personalized text messages making AI-generated texting conversations the latest tool political campaigns are using to connect.
articles.titleZh: 在中期选举之前收到竞选短信？背后可能有一个人工智能机器人
articles.summaryZh: 机器人被训练得像候选人一样，通过个性化短信与选民互动，使人工智能生成的短信对话成为政治竞选活动用来联系的最新工具。

contentParagraphs.en: In this file photo, a man using a smartphone passes through the arched windows at Grand Central Terminal in New York City on March 24, 2025.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在这张资料照片中，2025 年 3 月 24 日，一名男子使用智能手机穿过纽约市中央车站的拱形窗户。

contentParagraphs.en: Stay up to date with our Politics newsletter, sent weekly.
contentParagraphs.zh: 随时了解我们每周发送的政治时事通讯。

contentParagraphs.en: AI-powered platforms are training bots to sound like political candidates in text messages, holding personalized conversations with thousands of potential voters simultaneously. The bots are also gathering data, learning what each voter wants from their representatives and using that information to shape future campaign messaging.
contentParagraphs.zh: 人工智能驱动的平台正在训练机器人在短信中听起来像政治候选人，同时与数千名潜在选民进行个性化对话。这些机器人还收集数据，了解每个选民希望从其代表那里得到什么，并利用这些信息来塑造未来的竞选信息。

contentParagraphs.en: Aaron Sheeks, the CEO of Akillion, an AI platform that lets people run their own Large Language Models or bots, said many of his current clients are running for political office.
contentParagraphs.zh: Akillion 是一个让人们运行自己的大型语言模型或机器人的人工智能平台，其首席执行官亚伦·谢克斯 (Aaron Sheeks) 表示，他目前的许多客户都在竞选政治职位。

contentParagraphs.en: "Our goal is to put the microphone back in the hand of the voter," said Sheeks. "We're giving agencies and political campaigns the ability to have a trained AI employee that can go back and forth and answer questions on police reform or education or tax changes."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们的目标是将麦克风重新交到选民手中，”谢克斯说。 “我们正在为各机构和政治竞选活动提供训练有素的人工智能员工的能力，他们可以来回回答有关警察改革、教育或税收变化的问题。”

contentParagraphs.en: Some in the broader political text messaging industry say generative AI's ability to answer voter's questions and gather data about their concerns is going to be revolutionary for campaigns; others say political text messages are a limited — and annoying — tool and using AI won't improve it. While it's difficult to ascertain how many campaigns are using generative AI to text voters this election cycle, experts say Republicans have been adapting to AI faster than Democrats.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一些更广泛的政治短信行业人士表示，生成式人工智能能够回答选民的问题并收集有关他们担忧的数据，这对于竞选活动来说将是革命性的；其他人则表示，政治短信是一种有限且令人讨厌的工具，使用人工智能并不能改善它。虽然很难确定本次选举周期有多少竞选活动使用生成式人工智能向选民发短信，但专家表示，共和党人适应人工智能的速度比民主党人更快。

contentParagraphs.en: "My belief is that this is going to make campaigns more interactive, more responsive and more personalized," said Eric Wilson, a Republican strategist and the director of the Center for Campaign Innovation, a nonprofit that encourages conservative campaigns to adopt new technology. He said that generative AI "helps campaigns do more with less."
contentParagraphs.zh: 共和党战略家、竞选创新中心主任埃里克·威尔逊（Eric Wilson）表示：“我相信，这将使竞选活动更具互动性、响应性更强、更加个性化。”该非营利组织鼓励保守派竞选活动采用新技术。他说，生成式人工智能“帮助竞选活动用更少的钱做更多的事”。

contentParagraphs.en: In almost all cases the first text message sent to voters is written and sent by a human, Wilson said. The AI steps in when the recipient engages.
contentParagraphs.zh: 威尔逊说，几乎在所有情况下，发送给选民的第一条短信都是由人编写和发送的。当接收者参与时，人工智能就会介入。

contentParagraphs.en: The era of the long political text message is over, said Tom Carroll, the CEO of Convos, an AI-powered text messaging platform. Convos guides campaigns and their bots to say a sentence, introduce themselves and then ask a question to initiate a conversation.
contentParagraphs.zh: 人工智能短信平台 Convos 的首席执行官汤姆·卡罗尔 (Tom Carroll) 表示，冗长的政治短信时代已经结束。 Convos 引导活动及其机器人说一句话、介绍自己，然后提出问题来发起对话。

contentParagraphs.en: "What we're offering is the greatest volunteer you've ever had," said Carroll. "They'll respond within 30 seconds, in any language, cutting directly to the question that the person is asking."
contentParagraphs.zh: 卡罗尔说：“我们提供的是有史以来最伟大的志愿者。” “他们会在 30 秒内以任何语言做出回应，直接切入对方所问的问题。”

contentParagraphs.en: Convos launched last year, Carroll said, and helped with 10 political campaigns. This year they're aiming to work with over 100 campaigns; so far, they've hit roughly half of that target.
contentParagraphs.zh: 卡罗尔说，Convos 于去年推出，并帮助开展了 10 场政治活动。今年他们的目标是与 100 多个活动合作；到目前为止，他们已经实现了大约一半的目标。

contentParagraphs.en: Marty Santalucia, a partner at Vector Political, which is focused on generative-AI texting, said bots excel in engaging voters and "in some cases, we have people talk to our agent for hours." About 5-10% of people respond to texts and about 10-20% of those engage in 10 or more texts.
contentParagraphs.zh: 专注于生成人工智能短信的 VectorPolitical 的合伙人马蒂·桑塔卢西亚 (Marty Santalucia) 表示，机器人在吸引选民方面表现出色，“在某些情况下，我们会让人们与我们的代理人交谈数小时”。大约 5-10% 的人会回复短信，其中大约 10-20% 的人会回复 10 条或更多短信。

contentParagraphs.en: "We've sent two and a half million text messages this year and had over 20,000 to 30,000 conversations," said Santalucia. "We're listening at a scale that campaigns have never listened at before."
contentParagraphs.zh: “今年我们发送了 250 万条短信，并进行了 20,000 至 30,000 多次对话，”桑塔露西亚说。 “我们正在以前所未有的规模倾听竞选活动。”

contentParagraphs.en: The rise of the campaign text message
contentParagraphs.zh: 竞选短信的兴起

contentParagraphs.en: The market of political texting greatly expanded in 2020 as candidates were struggling to connect with voters on a grassroots level, said Josh Justice, the CEO of Peerly, a peer-to-peer texting platform. Live phone callers and phone banks diminished as landlines died out. Door-to-door canvassing grew outdated as people grew suspicious of answering the door for strangers. And on social media, the relationship between candidates and voters is owned by the tech platform.
contentParagraphs.zh: 点对点短信平台 Peerly 的首席执行官乔什·贾斯蒂斯 (Josh Justice) 表示，由于候选人难以与基层选民建立联系，2020 年政治短信市场大幅扩张。随着固定电话的消失，现场电话呼叫者和电话银行也随之减少。由于人们开始怀疑替陌生人开门，挨家挨户拉票已经过时了。而在社交媒体上，候选人和选民之间的关系是由科技平台掌控的。

contentParagraphs.en: Justice thinks every single House or Senate campaign this year will send out text messages because it's one of the few ways to reach voters directly on a mass scale. It stays on voters' phones, and it doesn't have to compete with the algorithm for attention.
contentParagraphs.zh: 正义认为，今年众议院或参议院的每一次竞选活动都会发送短信，因为这是直接大规模接触选民的少数方式之一。它保留在选民的手机上，并且不必与算法竞争注意力。

contentParagraphs.en: Justice and others who work for traditional political texting companies said they have ethical concerns with generative AI political text messaging. AI can be used for data analysis or for guiding volunteers, he said, but campaigns should immediately reveal to voters that they are talking to persuasive bots.
contentParagraphs.zh: 正义和其他在传统政治短信公司工作的人表示，他们对生成人工智能政治短信存在道德问题。他说，人工智能可以用于数据分析或指导志愿者，但竞选活动应该立即向选民透露他们正在与有说服力的机器人交谈。

contentParagraphs.en: "I don't think it's ethical to use generative A.I. to communicate with voters," said Justice. "You can put a disclaimer on there, and that's going to make it a lot better. But that defeats the purpose of what everybody started out doing."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我认为使用生成人工智能与选民沟通是不道德的，”贾斯​​蒂斯说。 “你可以在那里放一个免责声明，这会让事情变得更好。但这违背了每个人开始做的事情的目的。”

contentParagraphs.en: He is particularly concerned about campaigns using this while laws regulating AI are still being put into place. Campaigns in North Dakota and California have to tell recipients if they are talking to virtual assistants in their first message. Campaigns in New Jersey may soon have to disclose when they use generative AI to provide election-related information to voters.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他特别担心在监管人工智能的法律仍在制定的情况下使用这一点的活动。北达科他州和加利福尼亚州的活动必须告诉收件人他们在第一条消息中是否正在与虚拟助理交谈。新泽西州的竞选活动可能很快就必须披露何时使用生成式人工智能向选民提供与选举相关的信息。

contentParagraphs.en: Nathan Rifkin, co-CEO at Scale to Win, a tech company that does grassroots organizing and fundraising for progressives, said the risk of using generative AI outweighs the benefits, including the chatbot giving false information.
contentParagraphs.zh: Scale to Win 是一家为进步人士进行草根组织和筹款的科技公司，其联合首席执行官内森·里夫金 (Nathan Rifkin) 表示，使用生成式人工智能的风险超过了好处，包括聊天机器人会提供虚假信息。

contentParagraphs.en: "Or you can lead AI chat bots to say some pretty horrific things," said Rifkin. "If that's in the voice of the candidate, that can lead to some bad ends."
contentParagraphs.zh: “或者你可以引导人工智能聊天机器人说出一些非常可怕的事情，”里夫金说。 “如果候选人这么说，那可能会导致一些不好的结局。”

contentParagraphs.en: Tech companies selling generative AI text messages to political candidates say their clients aren't keen on going public. Some of that is because candidates don't want to share their "secret sauce" said Marty Santalucia from Vector Political. He also admits another element is that it's "very muddy in terms of where public perception is going to fall on this tool."
contentParagraphs.zh: 向政治候选人出售生成式人工智能短信的科技公司表示，他们的客户并不热衷于上市。矢量政治公司的马蒂·桑塔卢西亚表示，部分原因是候选人不想分享他们的“秘密武器”。他还承认另一个因素是“公众对这个工具的看法非常模糊”。

contentParagraphs.en: A Pew Research Center survey showed Democrats are less confident than Republicans in the government's ability to regulate AI effectively. Democratic campaigns are more hesitant to try out new technology, while Republican campaigns are more energetic about experimentation, Santalucia said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 皮尤研究中心的一项调查显示，民主党人对政府有效监管人工智能的能力不如共和党人那么有信心。桑塔露西亚说，民主党竞选团队对尝试新技术更加犹豫，而共和党竞选团队则更加热衷于实验。

contentParagraphs.en: Wilson, who trains Republican campaign strategists and candidate
contentParagraphs.zh: 威尔逊，培训共和党竞选策略师和候选人

articles.title: Is the Assassin's Creed Black Flag remake worth the 13 year wait?
articles.summary: The BBC's Tom Gerken plays the much-anticipated pirate game which has been remade from the ground up.
articles.titleZh: 《刺客信条：黑旗》重制版值得 13 年的等待吗？
articles.summaryZh: BBC 的汤姆·格肯 (Tom Gerken) 扮演了这款备受期待的海盗游戏，该游戏已从头开始重制。

contentParagraphs.en: Image source, UbisoftImage caption, It's a mystical quest to the isle of Tortuga... amongst other placesByTom GerkenTechnology reporterYou don't need to look hard to find a film or TV fan who will say a reboot is never as good as the original.Yet gamers often beg studios to remake their favourite titles and give them a new lease of life with modern tech.Some 13 years after its original was launched, Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced has been released to cash in on leaps in graphics and gameplay.But is it worth the wait?What Will We Do With a Drunken Sailor?Image source, UbisoftImage caption, Early 18th Century Havana has received a significant uplift between the original game (left) and the remake (right)Assassin's Creed is one of the most popular franchises in gaming, having sold an estimated 230 million copies across its various iterations.I have played every single one of them and, like many gamers, would rank Black Flag among the best.That mainly comes down to its excellent setting - players take control of fictional Welsh pirate Edward Kenway in the Caribbean during the 1700s.While hardly the first game about piracy, it was by far the most successful. So it is no real surprise Ubisoft chose this as the first game in the series to remake.It opens with pirate ships in battle, swiftly followed with a lush tropical island on a sunny day. It all looks good, as you would expect from a big budget game in 2026.Image source, UbisoftImage caption, Do you think Edward's feeding the turtle arrr-tichokes?Early on, players are encouraged to dive underwater and are met with a fantastic coral landscape.The developers are keen to show off the fancy new graphics, and here the game holds its own.It is a lot brighter than the original, which suffered from something unaffectionately referred to as the "muddy era" of gaming (amongst other less publishable names), where developers used darker colours to help games look gritty and realistic.Released right at the tail end of this era, the original Black Flag was not the most egregious example of this - but still suffered somewhat.Image source, UbisoftImage caption, Sailing a ship looks a bit different in 2013 (left) and 2026 (right).Now, we have moved far beyond this "muddy era" - and the new game is filled with bright colours befitting of the Caribbean setting.Running Down to CubaIt is a game which, in hoping to live up to the original's legacy, photographs well.Andy Farrant, co-editor of the YouTube channel Outside Xbox, told the BBC he was excited to play it."I firmly believe Black Flag is the best Assassin's Creed game," he said.For Farrant, its only downsides had been "the boring modern day bits" which saw a pirate's life swapped out for meetings and water cooler chat in a Montreal office - a section of the original axed in its remake."The world and the characters of Black Flag is what made it so appealing," he said."The chance to dip back into that world with some shiny new visuals and more screentime for fan favourite characters like Edward Kenway, Anne Bonny and Stede Bonnet can only be a good thing."Image source, UbisoftImage caption, The new underwater sections, complete with coral reefs, add a new layer of exploration to the gameSome fans might even call Black Flag Resynced a love letter to the original, though more cynical gamers may be inclined to call it a cash grab.If it is a hit, there is no question it could mean big money for Ubisoft.It would deliver some relief, too, given the company began 2026 by closing two studios, cancelling six games and delaying seven others.Remakes are big business in the gaming industry - something gaming expert Christopher Dring said can come down to "financial necessity"."The big video games are taking longer to make, and to fill gaps in the schedules, you're getting major companies turn to older classics, dusting them off and sometimes updating them for a modern era," he said."These games are typically very successful... as an industry, this remake, remaster, nostalgia business has become big."The Worst Old Ship That Ever Did SailImage source, UbisoftImage caption, While not always the best-looking game, the original Black Flag had its momentsThe new game, much like the original, offers a wide variety of activities ranging from swashbuckling and boarding ships to exploring the islands of the Caribbean.And that is really at the heart of what made the original so good - you have a map, you have to pick a spot, and sail there in search of treasure.It is the same basic idea which made The Legend of Zelda: The Wind Waker a hit for Nintendo in the 2000s, and Sid Meier's Pirates a success before that.And in a world where Mario Kart costs £75 and the upcoming Grand Theft Auto VI costs £70, Assassin's Creed comes in at just £50.Fans will be relieved to see Ubisoft bucking the current trend in game pricing, though perhaps it is appropriate for a remake.Image source, UbisoftImage caption, A lush cavern in the remake shows off the lighting capabilities of modern consolesBut despite all the new assets and graphics, there is no escaping the fact it is based on a game first released in 2013.Ubisoft had to make lots of calls about what to reuse and change from the original.Welsh actor Matt Ryan's performance is excellent, but some of the initial game's animations - such as characters repeatedly biting and spitting out a cork from a bottle - might have been best left in the past.The game also suffers from a problem all too common in modern gaming: it holds your hand far too much.In the most extreme case, at one point it gave me less than 10 seconds to figure out a puzzle before a character spat out the solution.The combat also clearly draws from modern Assassin's Creed games, but preserves some of the timing-based battling of the original.It's a nice marriage between the two, albeit disappointing that some things were not recreated - such as the ability to wield your assassin's hidden blades in combat.That combination of m
contentParagraphs.zh: 图片来源，Ubisoft图片说明，这是对托尔图加岛的神秘探索......以及其他地方作者：汤姆·格肯科技记者你不需要费力寻找电影或电视迷，他们会说重启永远不如原版。然而游戏玩家经常恳求工作室重制他们最喜欢的游戏，并用现代技术赋予它们新的生命。原版发行大约 13 年后，《刺客信条：黑旗》重新同步已经发布，以在图形和游戏玩法上实现飞跃。但它值得等待吗？我们将如何处理醉水水手？图片来源，育碧图片说明，18 世纪早期的哈瓦那在原版游戏（左）和重制版（右）之间获得了显着提升。《刺客信条》是游戏中最受欢迎的系列之一，在其各个版本中已售出估计 2.3 亿份。我玩过其中的每一款，并且像许多人一样，我玩过其中的每一款。玩家们会将《黑旗》列为最佳游戏之一。这主要归功于其出色的设定 - 玩家在 1700 年代的加勒比海中控制虚构的威尔士海盗爱德华·肯威。虽然它不是第一款关于海盗的游戏，但它是迄今为止最成功的游戏。因此，育碧选择这款游戏作为该系列的第一款重制游戏也就不足为奇了。游戏以海盗船的战斗开场，紧接着是阳光明媚的日子里一座郁郁葱葱的热带岛屿。一切看起来都不错，正如你对 2026 年大预算游戏所期望的那样。图片来源，Ubisoft 图片说明，你认为爱德华在喂海龟吗？早期，玩家被鼓励潜入水下并遇到梦幻般的珊瑚景观。开发人员热衷于炫耀精美的新图形，而游戏在这方面拥有自己的特色。它比原版明亮得多，后者遭受了被毫不亲切地称为“泥泞时代”的影响游戏（以及其他不太公开的名称），开发人员使用较暗的颜色来帮助游戏看起来坚韧和现实。在这个时代的尾声发布的原始黑旗并不是最令人震惊的例子 - 但仍然受到了一些影响。图片来源，育碧图片说明，航行的船在 2013 年（左）和 2026 年（右）看起来有点不同。现在，我们已经远远超越了这个“泥泞的时代” - 新游戏充满了明亮的色彩适合加勒比海的背景。奔向古巴是一款希望能够继承原作遗产的游戏，它的拍照效果也很好。YouTube 频道 Outside Xbox 的联合编辑安迪·法兰特 (Andy Farrant) 告诉 BBC，他很高兴能玩到这款游戏。“我坚信《黑旗》是最好的《刺客信条》游戏。”他说。对于法兰特来说，它唯一的缺点是“无聊的现代生活”，海盗的生活被会议和饮水机取代了。他说：“《黑旗》的世界和角色让它如此吸引人。” 《黑旗》重新同步了一封原作的情书，尽管更多愤世嫉俗的游戏玩家可能倾向于称其为抢钱。如果它大受欢迎，毫无疑问这可能对育碧意味着大笔收入。考虑到该公司在 2026 年初关闭了两个工作室，取消了六款游戏并推迟了另外七款游戏，这也将带来一些缓解。重制版是游戏行业的大生意 - 游戏专家克里斯托弗·德林表示，这可以归结为“财务需要”。 “制作时间更长，为了填补时间表上的空白，你会让大公司转向旧的经典游戏，把它们掸掉，有时还会更新它们以适应现代时代，”他说。“这些游戏通常非常成功……作为一个行业，这种翻拍、重制、怀旧的生意已经变得很大。”各种各样的活动，从虚张声势和登船到探索加勒比海岛屿。这正是原作如此出色的核心——你有一张地图，你必须选择一个地点，然后航行到那里寻找宝藏。正是基于同样的基本理念，《塞尔达传说：风之杖》在 2000 年代成为任天堂的热门游戏，而席德·梅尔的海盗在此之前也取得了成功。售价 75 英镑，即将推出的《侠盗猎车手 VI》售价 70 英镑，《刺客信条》售价仅为 50 英镑。看到育碧逆着当前游戏定价趋势，粉丝们会松一口气，尽管它可能适合重制。图片来源，育碧图片说明，重制版中郁郁葱葱的洞穴展示了现代游戏机的照明功能。但是，尽管有所有新的资产和图形，但无可否认的是，它是基于 2017 年首次发布的游戏。 2013年。育碧不得不多次呼吁重用和改变原作的内容。威尔士演员马特·瑞安的表演非常出色，但最初游戏的一些动画——比如角色反复咬住并吐出瓶子中的软木塞——最好还是留在过去。这款游戏还面临着现代游戏中常见的一个问题：它握着你的手太多。在最极端的情况下，有一次它只给了我不到 10 秒的时间来计算在角色吐出解决方案之前先解决一个谜题。战斗也明显借鉴了现代刺客信条游戏，但保留了原版的一些基于时间的战斗。两者之间的结合很好，尽管令人失望的是有些东西没有重新创建 - 例如在战斗中挥舞刺客袖剑的能力。

articles.title: Will Trump Accounts deliver for American children?
articles.summary: The White House has hailed the project as giving new generations a stake in the American dream, but the scheme has its critics.
articles.titleZh: 特朗普账户能为美国儿童带来好处吗？
articles.summaryZh: 白宫称赞该项目让新一代在美国梦中占有一席之地，但该计划也遭到批评。

contentParagraphs.en: The accounts named after the president are available nationwide and can be created for anyone under the age of 18 with a valid social security number. Parents can simply download the app.Families, friends and employers can contribute up to $5,000 per year per child, who can access the funds when they turn 18.By law, the money must be invested in a low-cost index fund designed for long-term growth. But while the money grows tax free, withdrawals are subject to taxes and a possible 10% penalty if made before the age of 59 and a half.To avoid such a penalty, the money must be assigned to pay for certain things, such as higher education, buying or building a first home, or for personal emergency expenses.Trump Accounts add to other existing tax-efficient savings schemes that Americans can use for retirement, such as IRAs, or for educational purposes, such as 529 plans, which parents use to save for their children's college fees.According to a Congress report,, external Trump Accounts are a new form of traditional individual retirement account (IRA), but differ because of certain rules.While the White House has been keen to push the scheme, reaction to it has been split.The White House's argument is that Trump Accounts offer millions of children a way into stock ownership in the US, which it says has historically been "unevenly distributed, with many households - especially younger and lower‑income families - having little or no exposure".However, Will McBride, chief economist at the Tax Foundation think tank, says the scheme is too complicated to sign up to, which will lead, in his view to a "minority that benefits".He suggests those that will take advantage will be the parents of children who are "relatively well-informed, relatively well-off, relatively tuned in [and] have their act together".However, Andy Blocker, head of policy, regulatory and government relations at financial services firm Edward Jones, believes the $1,000 contribution for babies born during Trump's second term in office will remove a "barrier of having nothing to start with"."If by year-end more families have a clear on-ramp to begin saving and investing for their children's financial futures, that's success," he suggests.Adam Michel, director of tax policy studies at the Cato Institute, says the idea of the scheme is admirable, but warns it might "not live up to the rhetoric".He says the main benefit is the $1,000 starting subsidy but suggests many families would be better off using existing savings accounts.He also points out barriers such as penalties for early withdrawal, as seen for other savings accounts, adding that lower-income children may feel compelled to take the money out when they turn 18 to "help make ends meet", and therefore have to pay a penalty. "Trump Accounts do not fix that problem."
contentParagraphs.zh: 以总统命名的账户在全国范围内开放，任何 18 岁以下且拥有有效社会安全号码的人都可以创建。家长只需下载该应用程序即可。家庭、朋友和雇主每年可为每个孩子捐款最多 5,000 美元，年满 18 岁时即可获得这些资金。根据法律，这笔钱必须投资于专为长期增长而设计的低成本指数基金。但是，虽然这笔钱增长免税，但如果在 59 岁半之前提款，则需要缴纳税款，并可能面临 10% 的罚款。为了避免这种罚款，这笔钱必须用于支付某些事情，例如高等教育、购买或建造第一套房子，或用于个人紧急开支。特朗普账户增加了美国人可以用于退休的其他现有节税储蓄计划，例如 IRA，或用于教育目的，例如 529 计划，父母可以用它来储蓄根据国会报告，外部特朗普账户是传统个人退休账户（IRA）的一种新形式，但由于某些规则而有所不同。虽然白宫一直热衷于推动该计划，但对此的反应却各有不同。白宫的论点是，特朗普账户为数百万儿童提供了获得美国股票所有权的途径，白宫表示，这种账户历来“分布不均，许多家庭，尤其是年轻和低收入家庭，几乎没有或根本没有股票所有权”。然而，税务基金会智囊团首席经济学家威尔·麦克布莱德表示，该计划过于复杂，无法签署，在他看来，这将导致“少数人受益”。他认为，受益者将是孩子的父母，他们“消息灵通，相对富裕，相对协调[并且]共同行动”。然而，金融服务公司爱德华·琼斯的政策、监管和政府关系主管安迪·布洛克尔认为，为特朗普第二任期内出生的婴儿提供 1000 美元捐款，将消除“一无所有的障碍”。他表示，“如果到年底，更多家庭有明确的入口，开始为孩子的财务未来进行储蓄和投资，那就成功了。”好处是1,000美元的起始补贴，但建议许多家庭使用现有的储蓄账户会更好。他还指出了一些障碍，例如提前取款的处罚，就像其他储蓄账户一样，并补充说，低收入的孩子可能会觉得在18岁的时候不得不把钱取出来“帮助维持收支平衡”，因此必须支付罚款。 “特朗普账户并不能解决这个问题。”

articles.title: Trump administration rolls back a key protection for imperiled wildlife
articles.summary: The Trump administration finalized a rule Friday that changes how agencies enforce the Endangered Species Act. The administration narrowed the definition of "harm" under the landmark law.
articles.titleZh: 特朗普政府取消了对濒危野生动物的关键保护
articles.summaryZh: 特朗普政府周五敲定了一项规则，改变了各机构执行《濒危物种法》的方式。政府根据具有里程碑意义的法律缩小了“伤害”的定义。

contentParagraphs.en: Banners of former President George Washington and President Donald Trump hang above an entrance to the Department of the Interior, Thursday, June 25, 2026, in Washington.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 6 月 25 日星期四，华盛顿，前总统乔治·华盛顿和唐纳德·特朗普总统的横幅悬挂在内政部入口上方。

contentParagraphs.en: PHOENIX — The Trump administration finalized a rule Friday that changes how agencies enforce the Endangered Species Act and eliminates a key protection for imperiled wildlife against logging, oil drilling and other activities.
contentParagraphs.zh: 菲尼克斯——特朗普政府周五敲定了一项规则，该规则改变了各机构执行《濒危物种法》的方式，并取消了对濒临灭绝的野生动物免受伐木、石油钻探和其他活动的关键保护。

contentParagraphs.en: The administration narrowed the definition of "harm" under the landmark law — a change with broad implications.
contentParagraphs.zh: 政府根据这项具有里程碑意义的法律缩小了“伤害”的定义——这一变化具有广泛的影响。

contentParagraphs.en: For decades, the government defined harm broadly to include encroachments on places with threatened and endangered animals. The change announced Friday would allow oil and gas drilling, mining, logging and and other development on critical wildlife habitats so long as the animals themselves aren't killed or injured.
contentParagraphs.zh: 几十年来，政府对伤害的定义很广泛，包括对受威胁和濒临灭绝动物的地区的侵犯。周五宣布的这一变化将允许在重要野生动物栖息地进行石油和天然气钻探、采矿、伐木和其他开发，只要动物本身没有死亡或受伤。

contentParagraphs.en: Environmentalists warned the move could cause some species to go extinct by opening the door to habitat destruction. Industry representatives and their Republican allies have long argued the landmark 1973 environmental law is wielded too broadly, to the detriment of economic growth.
contentParagraphs.zh: 环保人士警告称，此举可能会导致栖息地遭到破坏，从而导致一些物种灭绝。行业代表及其共和党盟友长期以来一直认为，具有里程碑意义的 1973 年环境法的适用范围过于广泛，不利于经济增长。

contentParagraphs.en: Administration officials said they were returning the law to its original intent, following a 2024 Supreme Court decision that limited the authority of federal agencies to interpret environmental statutes passed by Congress. They described the government's prior definition of harm as an intrusion on private property rights.
contentParagraphs.zh: 政府官员表示，继最高法院 2024 年做出一项限制联邦机构解释国会通过的环境法规的权力之后，他们正在让该法律回归其初衷。他们将政府先前对损害的定义描述为对私有财产权的侵犯。

contentParagraphs.en: It's among a suite of changes to wildlife protections that officials have pursued under President Donald Trump.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这是唐纳德·特朗普总统领导下的官员们对野生动物保护采取的一系列改革之一。

contentParagraphs.en: "For years, federal agencies abused the ESA to obstruct lawful land use and burden American families and businesses," Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said in a statement.
contentParagraphs.zh: 内政部长道格·布尔古姆在一份声明中表示：“多年来，联邦机构滥用欧洲航天局阻碍合法土地使用，给美国家庭和企业带来负担。”

contentParagraphs.en: The change was first proposed in April 2025 and environmentalists fought unsuccessfully to block it. Habitat destruction is the biggest cause of extinction, according to wildlife advocates.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这一改变于 2025 年 4 月首次提出，环保人士极力阻止，但未能成功。野生动物倡导者表示，栖息地破坏是灭绝的最大原因。

contentParagraphs.en: "This is one of the most horrific attempts to harm wildlife in American history and a gift to the oil barons and foreign mining companies," said Aaron Weiss, the executive director of the Center for Western Priorities.
contentParagraphs.zh: 西方优先事项中心执行主任亚伦·韦斯表示：“这是美国历史上最可怕的伤害野生动物的行为之一，也是送给石油大亨和外国矿业公司的一份礼物。”

contentParagraphs.en: The Endangered Species Act is credited with bringing back iconic animals — including the bald eagle, American alligator and California condor — from the brink of extinction.
contentParagraphs.zh: 《濒危物种法案》被誉为将秃鹰、美洲鳄和加州秃鹰等标志性动物从濒临灭绝的边缘拯救回来。

contentParagraphs.en: Republicans rolled back several provisions of the law in Trump's first term, only to have those moves reversed under Democratic President Joe Biden.
contentParagraphs.zh: 共和党人在特朗普的第一个任期内推翻了该法律的几项条款，但在民主党总统乔·拜登的领导下又推翻了这些举措。

articles.title: What a Monopoly importer learned when it tried to make things in the U.S.A.
articles.summary: After getting hit with tariffs for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva decided to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States. This is what he learned.
articles.titleZh: 垄断进口商在尝试在美国生产产品时学到了什么
articles.summaryZh: 在因销售的进口棋盘游戏受到关税打击后，乔纳森席尔瓦决定看看是否可以在美国制作大富翁游戏的版本。这就是他学到的。

contentParagraphs.en: In an effort to sidestep President Trump's tariffs, the WS Game Company decided to build a special edition Monopoly game in the United States. But the experiment almost didn't pass go.
contentParagraphs.zh: 为了规避特朗普总统的关税，WS 游戏公司决定在美国制作一款特别版大富翁游戏。但实验差点没通过。

contentParagraphs.en: WS Game Company
contentParagraphs.zh: WS游戏公司

contentParagraphs.en: WS Game Company
contentParagraphs.zh: WS游戏公司

contentParagraphs.en: The board game Monopoly has always taught some important economic lessons: The benefits of owning real estate. The profit potential of railroad mergers. The value of a get-out-of-jail-free card.
contentParagraphs.zh: 棋盘游戏《大富翁》一直在教导一些重要的经济教训：拥有房地产的好处。铁路合并的利润潜力。一张出狱卡的价值。

contentParagraphs.en: Now a special edition of the board game is teaching a new lesson—about how hard it is to make things in the USA.
contentParagraphs.zh: 现在，一款特别版的棋盘游戏正在给人们上一课——关于在美国制造东西有多么困难。

contentParagraphs.en: The game is being marketed by the WS Game Company, which produces most of its high-end board games in China, just like almost every other toy maker.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该游戏由 WS Game Company 销售，该公司大部分高端棋盘游戏都在中国生产，就像​​几乎所有其他玩具制造商一样。

contentParagraphs.en: After getting hit with a seven-figure tariff bill last year, CEO Jonathan Silva decided to see if it was possible to produce a profitable board game in the United States.
contentParagraphs.zh: 去年遭受七位数关税的打击后，首席执行官乔纳森席尔瓦决定看看是否有可能在美国制作一款有利可图的棋盘游戏。

contentParagraphs.en: He opted for a custom version of Monopoly, pegged to the country's 250th birthday. But the experiment almost didn't pass go. One big problem: No dice.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他选择了定制版的《大富翁》，与该国 250 岁生日挂钩。但实验差点没通过。一个大问题：没有骰子。

contentParagraphs.en: "We turned over every single leaf trying to find someone who would make 10,000 dice for us in the U.S.," Silva says. "It requires special machinery. It requires investment. And that type of stuff just can't happen on a random Tuesday and be ready in a couple of months."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们翻遍了每一片叶子，试图在美国找到一个能为我们制作 10,000 个骰子的人，”席尔瓦说。 “它需要特殊的机械。它需要投资。这种事情不可能在周二随机发生并在几个月内准备好。”

contentParagraphs.en: Silva ultimately had to settle for imported dice.
contentParagraphs.zh: 席尔瓦最终不得不接受进口骰子。

contentParagraphs.en: He was able to find the rest of what he needed domestically, but it wasn't easy. A former Hasbro factory in Massachusetts prints the Monopoly board. A company called Pioneer Packaging makes the tray that holds he Monopoly money. And a small business in Indiana cranked out custom metal game tokens, in all-American shapes like a cowboy hat, a covered wagon and an apple pie.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他能够在国内找到他需要的其他东西，但这并不容易。马萨诸塞州的一家前孩之宝工厂负责印刷大富翁棋盘。一家名为先锋包装公司（Pioneer Packaging）生产装有大富翁钱的托盘。印第安纳州的一家小企业生产了定制的金属游戏代币，其形状具有牛仔帽、敞篷马车和苹果派等美国式的形状。

contentParagraphs.en: Stateline Industries in Liberty, Indiana fabricated the custom game pieces for the Monopoly Americana edition, with special shapes like a cowboy hat and an apple pie.
contentParagraphs.zh: 印第安纳州自由城的斯泰特莱恩工业公司为《美国大富翁》版本制作了定制游戏部件，其中包括牛仔帽和苹果派等特殊形状。

contentParagraphs.en: WS Game Company
contentParagraphs.zh: WS游戏公司

contentParagraphs.en: WS Game Company
contentParagraphs.zh: WS游戏公司

contentParagraphs.en: Just assembling all those different players took more than a year, so Silva missed the first half of the 250th birthday selling season. And the cost to manufacture the games — which retail for $80 — was at least double what it would have been in China.
contentParagraphs.zh: 仅仅组装所有这些不同的球员就花了一年多的时间，因此席尔瓦错过了 250 岁生日销售季的前半段。这些游戏的制作成本（零售价为 80 美元）至少是在中国的两倍。

contentParagraphs.en: "When I place a purchase order in China, they have all those capabilities under one roof," Silva says. "For one item, it took up way too much of our resources and time to bring it to market."
contentParagraphs.zh: “当我在中国下订单时，他们拥有所有这些能力，”席尔瓦说。 “对于一件产品来说，我们花费了太多的资源和时间来将其推向市场。”

contentParagraphs.en: Why so many things are made in China
contentParagraphs.zh: 为什么这么多东西是中国制造

contentParagraphs.en: There's a reason nearly 80% of all toys and games sold in the U.S. are made in China. That country has spent decades building a factory ecosystem to supply not only finished products, but all the specialized parts that go into them.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在美国销售的所有玩具和游戏中，近 80% 都是中国制造，这是有原因的。该国花了几十年的时间建立了一个工厂生态系统，不仅供应成品，还供应成品的所有专用零件。

contentParagraphs.en: "That's why the re-shoring and the looking at bringing it back into the U.S. or even looking at other countries and moving it is not as easy as it sounds," says Greg Ahearn, president and CEO of the The Toy Association, an industry trade group.
contentParagraphs.zh: 行业贸易组织玩具协会主席兼首席执行官格雷格·埃赫恩表示：“这就是为什么回流以及考虑将其带回美国，甚至考虑将其转移到其他国家并不像听起来那么容易。”

contentParagraphs.en: Ahearn says it makes sense for the U.S. to manufacture some strategically important products, but probably not when it comes to most toys and games, which tend to carry both low prices and low profit margins.
contentParagraphs.zh: 埃赫恩表示，美国生产一些具有重要战略意义的产品是有意义的，但对于大多数玩具和游戏来说可能并不合理，因为这些产品往往价格低廉，利润率也较低。

contentParagraphs.en: "Even if you could, who in their right mind would take their capital and invest it into creating a toy manufacturing plant?" Ahearn says. "Of all the things you could pick, we'd probably be pretty low on that list."
contentParagraphs.zh: “即使可以，谁心智正常会拿自己的资金投资创建一家玩具制造厂呢？”埃赫恩说道。 “在你可以选择的所有事情中，我们可能在这个名单上排得很靠后。”

contentParagraphs.en: Instead, the toy industry is lobbying to get a carve-out from future tariffs. The new U.S.—China Board of Trade is considering allowing up to $30 billion worth of Chinese products to enter the U.S. tariff-free. But toys are competing for the tax break with shoes, apparel and many other products.
contentParagraphs.zh: 相反，玩具行业正在游说以获得未来关税的豁免。新成立的美中贸易委员会正在考虑允许价值高达300亿美元的中国产品免关税进入美国。但玩具正在与鞋子、服装和许多其他产品竞争税收减免。

contentParagraphs.en: For now, Silva is busy marketing his Made in the U.S.A. Monopoly game. But he's still making the rest of his company's board games in China. And that's not likely to change.
contentParagraphs.zh: 目前，席尔瓦正忙于营销他的美国制造的大富翁游戏。但他仍在中国制作公司的其余棋盘游戏。这不太可能改变。

contentParagraphs.en: "We're really good at a lot of great things here in America." Silva says. "But we're not really great at making certain items that are consumable goods. And that's OK."
contentParagraphs.zh: “在美国，我们确实擅长做很多伟大的事情。”席尔瓦说。 “但我们并不擅长生产某些消费品。但这没关系。”

contentParagraphs.en: He's now awaiting a shipment from China of about $6 million worth of games for the upcoming holiday season. He has no idea what the tariff bill might be. But he's prepared to roll the dice.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他现在正在等待从中国发货的价值约 600 万美元的游戏，以供即将到来的假期使用。他不知道关税法案可能是什么。但他已经准备好掷骰子了。

articles.title: Apple sues OpenAI, its employees claiming theft of trade secrets
articles.summary: Apple said in a Friday lawsuit that OpenAI’s nascent hardware business is “rotten to its core.”
articles.titleZh: 苹果起诉 OpenAI，其员工声称窃取商业机密
articles.summaryZh: 苹果在周五的诉讼中表示，OpenAI 新兴的硬件业务“已经烂到了核心”。

contentParagraphs.en: Apple has accused OpenAI of gaining access to valuable inside information through the hiring of its former employees.In a federal lawsuit filed on Friday, Apple sued the artificial intelligence (AI) company, two of its employees, as well as io Products, claiming it has engaged in "a pattern of theft" of Apple's confidential product development and related work.At least two long-time Apple workers who left the company to join OpenAI allegedly took part in this pattern by, in part, emailing themselves internal Apple information.Drew Pusateri, a spokesman for OpenAI, told the BBC: "We have no interest in other companies' trade secrets."Pusateri added that the company, which is currently reviewing Apple's complaint, is "focused on building innovative technology that empowers people everywhere."An Apple spokesman told the BBC the lawsuit is the result of "significant evidence."It represents a major shift in relations between Apple and OpenAI, creator of the popular AI chatbot ChatGPT.Tim Cook, Apple's outgoing CEO, had added ChatGPT into Apple devices as the company was looking to offer more AI features.This year, Apple shifted more of its AI features to run on Google's Gemini model and tools.Yet, when Cook revealed in April that he was stepping down, Sam Altman, co-founder and CEO of OpenAI, publicly praised him as "a legend", adding he was "very thankful for everything he has done".Now, Apple is accusing OpenAI of undertaking a "strategy to extract Apple's confidential information".Along with OpenAI, Apple is suing io Products, the design startup founded by Jony Ive, a long-time Apple executive. OpenAI acquired the company last year.It is also suing Chang Liu, a senior electrical engineer who worked at Apple for eight years, and Tang Yew Tan, a vice president of design for iPhone and Apple Watch who spent 24 years with the company. Tan is now OpenAI's chief hardware officer.Through these former employees and their access to "sensitive projects, trusted partner relationships, proprietary manufacturing techniques, and unreleased products," Apple claims OpenAI has been able to glean details of its product plans and operations.Apple added that when OpenAI interviews current Apple employees for potential jobs the company allegedly tries to extract further information from them.OpenAI interviewers allegedly have told the prospective hires to "bring 'actual parts' as 'props' from Apple for 'show and tell'" during their interview.Apple accused all of the parties it was suing of "acting in concert and as an enterprise, exploiting Apple's confidential information to advance OpenAI's efforts to enter the consumer hardware market".OpenAI is expected to release its first hardware product, a type of keyboard to be used with its AI tools, this month.It is also planning to become a publicly traded company.Apple said in its lawsuit that, because OpenAI's "misconduct is normalized and exemplified by leadership" its "nascent hardware business now rests on the shakiest of foundations, rotten to its core by its illegal reliance on misappropriated trade secrets".The company also said that it had attempted to discuss it's concerns with OpenAI in February, but was ultimately ignored.Apple has asked the court to immediately prohibit OpenAI from obtaining or using any alleged confidential information and it is seeking unspecified monetary damages.
contentParagraphs.zh: 苹果指控 OpenAI 通过雇用前员工获取有价值的内部信息。在周五提起的联邦诉讼中，苹果起诉这家人工智能 (AI) 公司、其两名员工以及 io Products，声称该公司参与了“盗窃”苹果机密产品开发和相关工作的行为。据称，至少两名离开公司加入 OpenAI 的长期苹果员工参与了这种行为，部分原因是通过电子邮件向自己发送苹果内部信息。 OpenAI 的发言人告诉 BBC：“我们对其他公司的商业秘密没有兴趣。”Pusateri 补充说，该公司目前正在审查苹果的投诉，“专注于构建创新技术，为世界各地的人们提供帮助。”苹果发言人告诉 BBC，这起诉讼是“重大证据”的结果。这代表了苹果和流行人工智能聊天机器人 ChatGPT 的创建者 OpenAI 之间关系的重大转变。苹果即将离任的首席执行官蒂姆·库克已将 ChatGPT 添加到了 OpenAI 中。随着苹果公司寻求提供更多人工智能功能，苹果设备将更多人工智能功能转移到谷歌的 Gemini 模型和工具上运行。然而，当库克在 4 月份透露自己将辞职时，OpenAI 联合创始人兼首席执行官 Sam Altman 公开称赞他是“一个传奇”，并补充说他“非常感谢他所做的一切”。现在，苹果指责 OpenAI 采取了“提取苹果机密信息的策略”。正在起诉 io Products，这是一家由长期担任苹果高管的 Jony Ive 创立的设计初创公司。 OpenAI 去年收购了该公司。 它还起诉了在苹果工作了 8 年的高级电气工程师 Chang Liu，以及在该公司工作了 24 年的 iPhone 和 Apple Watch 设计副总裁 Tang Yew Tan。谭现在是 OpenAI 的首席硬件官。通过这些前员工以及他们对“敏感项目、值得信赖的合作伙伴关系、专有制造技术和未发布产品”的访问，苹果声称 OpenAI 已经能够收集其产品计划和运营的详细信息。苹果补充说，当 OpenAI 面试现任苹果员工以获取潜在工作时，据称该公司试图从他们那里获取更多信息。据称，OpenAI 面试官告诉潜在员工“从苹果带来‘实际零件’作为‘道具’，用于‘展示和展示’。”苹果公司在接受采访时指责所有被起诉方“一致行动，并作为一家企业，利用苹果公司的机密信息来推动 OpenAI 进军消费硬件市场的努力”。OpenAI 预计将于本月发布其首款硬件产品，一种与其 AI 工具一起使用的键盘。它还计划成为一家上市公司。苹果公司在诉讼中表示，由于 OpenAI 的“不当行为已正常化，并以领导层为例证”，其“新生”硬件业务现在建立在最不稳定的基础上，其核心因非法依赖盗用的商业秘密而腐烂”。该公司还表示，它曾在 2 月份试图与 OpenAI 讨论其担忧，但最终被忽视。苹果已要求法院立即禁止 OpenAI 获取或使用任何所谓的机密信息，并正在寻求未具体说明的金钱赔偿。

articles.title: Apple sues OpenAI, alleging artificial intelligence company stole trade secrets
articles.summary:
Suit claims OpenAI poached Apple workers, coaxing them to share confidential material in bid to create hardware
Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI on Friday alleging the artificial intelligence firm stole company trade secrets in a move to create its own hardware device.
The su
articles.titleZh: 苹果起诉OpenAI，指控人工智能公司窃取商业机密
articles.summaryZh:
诉讼称 OpenAI 挖走了苹果员工，诱骗他们分享机密材料以制造硬件
苹果周五对 OpenAI 提起诉讼，指控这家人工智能公司在创建自己的硬件设备的过程中窃取了公司的商业机密。
苏

contentParagraphs.en: Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI on Friday alleging the artificial intelligence firm stole company trade secrets in a move to create its own hardware device.The suit claims OpenAI poached Apple employees, coaxing them to hand over confidential material, product designs and other tightly held information.“Recently, significant evidence has emerged suggesting individuals employed by OpenAI wrongfully took Apple’s secret and confidential information regarding our unreleased technologies, processes and products,” an Apple spokesperson said in an email.Drew Pusateri, a spokesperson for OpenAI, said the company was reviewing the court filing. “We have no interest in other companies’ trade secrets,” he added. “We remain focused on building innovative technology that empowers people everywhere.”Apple’s lawsuit is a sharp turnaround for the two tech giants, which announced a major partnership in 2024. That deal involved Apple integrating OpenAI’s chatbot, ChatGPT, into the operating systems for iPhones, iPads and Macs. But when Apple showcased its revamped voice assistant Siri last month, its AI component was based on Google’s Gemini AI model, rather than ChatGPT.Tensions between the two companies began to simmer last year when OpenAI spent $6.4bn to acquire a hardware startup founded by former Apple design guru Jony Ive, indicating that the AI titan was foraying into hardware. Ive’s startup, io Products, is also named in Apple’s lawsuit.“OpenAI’s nascent hardware business now rests on the shakiest of foundations, rotten to its core by its illegal reliance on misappropriated trade secrets,” Apple wrote in its complaint.The suit alleges several former Apple employees joined OpenAI, taking company trade secrets with them. Tang Yew Tan, OpenAI’s chief hardware officer and a former vice-president at Apple, is named in the suit. Apple alleges that Tan took information about Apple suppliers with him to OpenAI and encouraged interviewees at OpenAI to divulge confidential company information.“He has directed job candidates still working for Apple to bring ‘actual parts’ from Apple to their interviews for ‘show and tell’ sessions in which he and his team at OpenAI can elicit still more Apple confidential information,” reads Apple’s complaint.Chang Liu, another former Apple employee named in the suit who was hired at OpenAI, is accused of taking an Apple laptop with him when he left. Apple alleges that Liu used an authentication bug to breach the company’s internal network and downloaded “dozens of Apple’s confidential hardware-related files”.The Apple spokesperson said: “Our teams are constantly developing breakthrough technologies to create the best products and services in the world, and protecting their work and intellectual property is something we take very seriously.”The company is seeking damages and a court order that would block OpenAI from possessing or using its trade secrets.
contentParagraphs.zh: 苹果周五对 OpenAI 提起诉讼，指控这家人工智能公司窃取公司商业机密，以创建自己的硬件设备。诉讼称 OpenAI 挖走苹果员工，诱骗他们交出机密材料、产品设计和其他严格掌握的信息。苹果发言人在一封电子邮件中表示：“最近，有重要证据表明，OpenAI 雇用的个人错误地获取了苹果有关我们未发布的技术、流程和产品的秘密和机密信息。” OpenAI 表示，该公司正在审查法庭文件。 “我们对其他公司的商业秘密没有兴趣，”他补充道。 “我们仍然专注于构建创新技术，为世界各地的人们提供帮助。” 苹果的诉讼对这两家科技巨头来说是一个巨大的转变，这两家科技巨头宣布将在 2024 年建立重大合作伙伴关系。该交易涉及苹果将 OpenAI 的聊天机器人 ChatGPT 集成到 iPhone、iPad 和 Mac 的操作系统中。但当苹果上个月展示其改进的语音助手 Siri 时，其人工智能组件是基于谷歌的 Gemini AI 模型，而不是 ChatGPT。去年，当 OpenAI 斥资 64 亿美元收购了前苹果设计大师 Jony Ive 创立的一家硬件初创公司时，两家公司之间的紧张关系开始升温，这表明这家人工智能巨头正在进军硬件领域。艾维的初创公司 io Products 也在苹果的诉讼中被点名。苹果在诉状中写道：“OpenAI 新兴的硬件业务现在建立在最不稳固的基础上，由于非法依赖盗用的商业机密而腐烂到了核心。”该诉讼称，几名前苹果员工加入了 OpenAI，并带走了公司的商业机密。诉讼中提到了 OpenAI 首席硬件官、苹果前副总裁 Tang Yew Tan。苹果公司称，谭向 OpenAI 提供了有关苹果供应商的信息，并鼓励 OpenAI 的受访者透露公司机密信息。苹果公司的诉状中写道：“他指示仍在为苹果公司工作的求职者将苹果公司的‘实际部件’带到他们的面试中进行‘展示和讲述’会议，这样他和他在 OpenAI 的团队就可以获取更多的苹果机密信息。”离开了。苹果公司声称，刘利用身份验证漏洞侵入了公司的内部网络，并下载了“数十个苹果公司的机密硬件相关文件”。苹果公司发言人表示：“我们的团队正在不断开发突破性技术，以创造世界上最好的产品和服务，保护他们的工作和知识产权是我们非常重视的事情。”该公司正在寻求损害赔偿和法院命令，以阻止 OpenAI 拥有或使用其商业秘密。
